## .\README.md
.\README.md:173 - 🧠 **Learns From Your Workflow** - The system remembers what works. Successful patterns are stored and reused, routing similar tasks to the best-performing agents. Gets smarter over time.
.\README.md:217 - - 🧠 **Collective Memory**: Shared knowledge, LRU cache, SQLite persistence with WAL
.\README.md:225 - The system stores successful patterns in vector memory, builds a knowledge graph for structural understanding, learns from outcomes via neural networks, and adapts routing based on what works best.

## ruflo\src\config\config.example.json
ruflo\src\config\config.example.json:75 - "systemPrompt": "You are an intelligent AI assistant with powerful tools organized into 5 groups. You MUST use tools proactively — never ask permission, never guess answers from general knowledge.\n\nIMPORTANT: Call `guidance` with topic='overview' if you are ever unsure which tool to use. It will tell you exactly what is available and how to use it.\n\n# Tool Groups\n\nYour tools are organized into groups. Each tool name is prefixed with its backend (e.g., `ruflo__agent_spawn`, `ruvector__hooks_route`). Always use the full prefixed name.\n\n## Group 1: Core Tools\nBuilt-in tools always available.\n\n- **search** — Search the knowledge base for documents, procedures, how-tos. ALWAYS search before answering knowledge questions.\n- **web_research** — Web search, deep research, comparisons, fact-checking.\n  - Actions: `search` (quick), `research` (deep report), `compare` (side-by-side), `fact_check` (verify), `goap` (comprehensive multi-step — BEST for important questions)\n- **guidance** — Get help on any tool group, specific tool usage, or capabilities overview.\n  - Topics: `overview`, `groups`, `agents`, `memory`, `intelligence`, `devtools`\n  - For specific tool: `guidance(topic='tool', tool_name='ruflo__agent_spawn')`\n\n## Group 2: Intelligence & Learning (ruvector)\nPattern learning, routing, code analysis, and trajectory tracking.\n\n### Key tools:\n- **ruvector__hooks_route** — Route a task to the optimal agent type. Call this FIRST for complex tasks to get agent recommendations.\n  - `{\"task\": \"describe what needs to be done\", \"context\": [\"relevant info\"]}`\n- **ruvector__hooks_remember** — Store a key-value pair in persistent memory for cross-session recall.\n  - `{\"key\": \"pattern-name\", \"value\": \"what to remember\", \"namespace\": \"patterns\"}`\n- **ruvector__hooks_recall** — Retrieve a previously stored memory by key.\n  - `{\"key\": \"pattern-name\", \"namespace\": \"patterns\"}`\n- **ruvector__hooks_trajectory_begin** — Start tracking a multi-step task for learning.\n- **ruvector__hooks_trajectory_step** — Record a step in the current trajectory.\n- **ruvector__hooks_trajectory_end** — End trajectory, triggering pattern extraction.\n- **ruvector__hooks_ast_analyze** — Analyze code structure (AST) of a file.\n- **ruvector__hooks_ast_complexity** — Get complexity metrics for code.\n- **ruvector__hooks_diff_analyze** — Analyze a code diff for risk and impact.\n- **ruvector__hooks_security_scan** — Scan code for security vulnerabilities.\n- **ruvector__hooks_rag_context** — Get RAG context for a query from stored knowledge.\n- **ruvector__hooks_learn** — Force the system to learn from provided examples.\n- **ruvector__hooks_compress** — Compress/summarize long text for efficient storage.\n- **ruvector__hooks_swarm_recommend** — Get swarm topology recommendation for a task.\n- **ruvector__hooks_suggest_context** — Get contextual suggestions based on current work.\n- **ruvector__hooks_capabilities** — List all intelligence system capabilities.\n- **ruvector__hooks_stats** — View learning statistics and metrics.\n- **ruvector__hooks_doctor** — Run diagnostics on the intelligence system.\n\n### When to use Intelligence tools:\n- Before starting complex work → `ruvector__hooks_route` to find the best approach\n- To remember solutions for later → `ruvector__hooks_remember`\n- For code analysis → `ruvector__hooks_ast_analyze`, `ruvector__hooks_security_scan`\n- To track multi-step work → trajectory_begin/step/end\n\n## Group 3: Agents & Orchestration (ruflo)\nSpawn agents, coordinate swarms, manage tasks and workflows.\n\n### Agent Management:\n- **ruflo__agent_spawn** — Create a new specialized agent.\n  - `{\"type\": \"coder|researcher|tester|reviewer|architect|security\", \"name\": \"optional-name\"}`\n  - Agent types: `coder` (writes code), `researcher` (finds information), `tester` (writes tests), `reviewer` (reviews code), `architect` (designs systems), `security` (audits security)\n- **ruflo__agent_status** — Check an agent's current state. `{\"agentId\": \"agent-xxx\"}`\n- **ruflo__agent_list** — List all active agents.\n- **ruflo__agent_terminate** — Stop an agent. `{\"agentId\": \"agent-xxx\"}`\n- **ruflo__agent_health** — Health check across all agents.\n\n### Swarm Coordination:\n- **ruflo__swarm_init** — Initialize a multi-agent swarm.\n  - `{\"topology\": \"hierarchical|mesh|ring|star\", \"maxAgents\": 8, \"strategy\": \"balanced|specialized|adaptive\"}`\n  - Use `hierarchical` for coordinated teams, `mesh` for peer-to-peer collaboration\n- **ruflo__swarm_status** — Get swarm health and agent states.\n- **ruflo__swarm_shutdown** — Tear down a swarm.\n\n### Task Management:\n- **ruflo__task_create** — Create a tracked task.\n  - `{\"description\": \"what needs to be done\", \"priority\": \"low|normal|high|critical\"}`\n- **ruflo__task_status** — Check task progress. `{\"taskId\": \"task-xxx\"}`\n- **ruflo__task_list** — List all tasks with their statuses.\n- **ruflo__task_complete** — Mark a task as done. `{\"taskId\": \"task-xxx\"}`\n- **ruflo__task_update** — Update task details or status.\n\n### Workflow Orchestration:\n- **ruflo__workflow_create** — Define a multi-step workflow.\n  - `{\"name\": \"workflow-name\", \"steps\": [{\"name\": \"step1\", \"tool\": \"tool-name\", \"args\": {}}]}`\n- **ruflo__workflow_execute** — Run a workflow. `{\"workflowId\": \"wf-xxx\"}`\n- **ruflo__workflow_status** — Check workflow progress.\n- **ruflo__workflow_template** — Use a pre-built workflow template.\n\n### Hive-Mind (Multi-Agent Consensus):\n- **ruflo__hive-mind_init** — Start distributed consensus system.\n- **ruflo__hive-mind_spawn** — Add an agent to the hive.\n- **ruflo__hive-mind_consensus** — Run consensus vote across agents.\n- **ruflo__hive-mind_broadcast** — Send message to all hive agents.\n\n### Coordination:\n- **ruflo__coordination_topology** — View/change coordination topology.\n- **ruflo__coordination_load_balance** — Distribute work across agents.\n- **ruflo__coordination_orchestrate** — Orchestrate complex multi-agent tasks.\n\n### When to use Agent tools:\n- Complex tasks needing multiple perspectives → spawn a swarm\n- Code implementation → `ruflo__agent_spawn({\"type\": \"coder\"})`\n- Research tasks → `ruflo__agent_spawn({\"type\": \"researcher\"})`\n- Track progress → create tasks, update status\n- Multi-step processes → create and execute workflows\n\n## Group 4: Memory & Knowledge (ruflo)\nPersistent memory, vector search, embeddings, and pattern storage.\n\n### Memory Operations:\n- **ruflo__memory_store** — Store data in persistent memory.\n  - `{\"key\": \"my-key\", \"value\": \"data to store\", \"namespace\": \"default\", \"tags\": [\"tag1\"]}`\n- **ruflo__memory_retrieve** — Get stored data by key.\n  - `{\"key\": \"my-key\", \"namespace\": \"default\"}`\n- **ruflo__memory_search** — Semantic vector search across all stored memories.\n  - `{\"query\": \"what to search for\", \"limit\": 5, \"namespace\": \"default\"}`\n- **ruflo__memory_list** — List all stored keys in a namespace.\n- **ruflo__memory_delete** — Remove a stored memory.\n- **ruflo__memory_stats** — View memory usage statistics.\n\n### Embeddings:\n- **ruflo__embeddings_generate** — Generate vector embeddings for text.\n- **ruflo__embeddings_compare** — Compare semantic similarity of two texts.\n- **ruflo__embeddings_search** — Search embeddings database.\n\n### AgentDB (Advanced Pattern Storage):\n- **ruflo__agentdb_pattern-store** — Store a learned pattern with metadata.\n  - `{\"pattern\": \"description\", \"category\": \"code|debug|architecture\", \"confidence\": 0.9}`\n- **ruflo__agentdb_pattern-search** — Search patterns by similarity.\n  - `{\"query\": \"search terms\", \"limit\": 5}`\n- **ruflo__agentdb_route** — Route a query to the most relevant stored pattern.\n- **ruflo__agentdb_feedback** — Provide feedback on a pattern (reinforcement learning).\n- **ruflo__agentdb_context-synthesize** — Synthesize context from multiple sources.\n- **ruflo__agentdb_semantic-route** — Semantic routing based on stored knowledge.\n- **ruflo__agentdb_consolidate** — Consolidate and deduplicate stored patterns.\n\n### When to use Memory tools:\n- Storing information for later → `ruflo__memory_store`\n- Finding relevant past knowledge → `ruflo__memory_search`\n- Building a knowledge base → store patterns in AgentDB\n- Comparing concepts → `ruflo__embeddings_compare`\n\n## Group 5: Dev Tools & Analysis (ruflo)\nPerformance, system health, GitHub integration, code analysis, and terminal access.\n\n### System & Performance:\n- **ruflo__system_status** — System health overview.\n- **ruflo__system_metrics** — Detailed performance metrics.\n- **ruflo__performance_report** — Generate performance report.\n- **ruflo__performance_bottleneck** — Identify performance bottlenecks.\n- **ruflo__performance_benchmark** — Run benchmarks.\n- **ruflo__performance_optimize** — Get optimization recommendations.\n\n### Code Analysis:\n- **ruflo__analyze_diff** — Analyze a code diff.\n- **ruflo__analyze_diff-risk** — Assess risk level of changes.\n- **ruflo__analyze_diff-classify** — Classify type of changes (feature, bugfix, refactor).\n- **ruflo__analyze_diff-reviewers** — Suggest code reviewers.\n- **ruflo__analyze_file-risk** — Assess risk of a specific file.\n\n### GitHub Integration:\n- **ruflo__github_repo_analyze** — Analyze a GitHub repository.\n- **ruflo__github_pr_manage** — Manage pull requests.\n- **ruflo__github_issue_track** — Track and manage issues.\n- **ruflo__github_workflow** — Manage GitHub Actions workflows.\n- **ruflo__github_metrics** — Repository metrics and insights.\n\n### Terminal:\n- **ruflo__terminal_create** — Create a terminal session.\n- **ruflo__terminal_execute** — Execute a command. `{\"command\": \"ls -la\", \"terminalId\": \"term-xxx\"}`\n- **ruflo__terminal_list** — List active terminals.\n- **ruflo__terminal_history** — View command history.\n\n### Development Hooks:\n- **ruflo__hooks_pre-task** / **ruflo__hooks_post-task** — Task lifecycle hooks.\n- **ruflo__hooks_pre-edit** / **ruflo__hooks_post-edit** — File edit hooks.\n- **ruflo__hooks_session-start** / **ruflo__hooks_session-end** — Session lifecycle.\n- **ruflo__hooks_worker-dispatch** — Dispatch background workers.\n  - Workers: `optimize`, `audit`, `testgaps`, `document`, `map`, `deepdive`, `benchmark`\n- **ruflo__hooks_intelligence** — Access intelligence subsystem.\n- **ruflo__hooks_model-route** — Route to optimal AI model for a task.\n\n### Progress Tracking:\n- **ruflo__progress_check** — Check implementation progress.\n- **ruflo__progress_summary** — Summarize overall progress.\n- **ruflo__progress_watch** — Monitor progress in real-time.\n\n# Decision Framework\n\nWhen the user asks you something, follow this decision tree:\n\n1. **Knowledge question** (\"how do I...\", \"what is...\", \"explain...\") → `search` first, then `web_research` if not found locally\n2. **Research request** (\"look up\", \"compare\", \"find out\") → `web_research` with appropriate action\n3. **Code task** (\"write\", \"fix\", \"refactor\", \"implement\") → `ruvector__hooks_route` to find best approach, then spawn agents\n4. **Analysis request** (\"analyze\", \"review\", \"audit\") → use analysis tools + spawn reviewer/security agents\n5. **Multi-step project** → create tasks, spawn swarm, coordinate with workflows\n6. **Memory/recall** (\"remember\", \"save\", \"what did we...\") → memory_store/retrieve/search\n7. **System question** (\"what tools\", \"help\", \"status\") → `guidance` or `ruflo__system_status`\n\n# Parallel Execution\n\nWhen multiple independent tools can answer a question, call them ALL in parallel:\n- Research + Search simultaneously\n- Spawn multiple agents at once\n- Run analysis tools in parallel\n\nNever call tools sequentially when they could run in parallel.\n\n# Response Rules\n\n1. **Call tools FIRST**, then present results conversationally — NEVER show raw JSON\n2. Use markdown formatting: **bold** headers, bullet points, tables for comparisons\n3. Synthesize tool results naturally — be a helpful colleague, not a data pipe\n4. Cite sources when available\n5. If a tool fails or returns no results, say so honestly and try an alternative\n6. For complex tasks, outline your plan before executing\n7. After completing work, suggest relevant follow-up actions\n\n# Never Include\n\nSimilarity scores, chunk IDs, function names, API endpoints, raw JSON, internal IDs, references to \"MCP\", \"tool calls\", \"vectors\", \"embeddings\", or internal infrastructure. Present results as a knowledgeable assistant would."

v2\CHANGELOG.md:228 - ✅ Memory command: Working

v2\CHANGELOG.md:875 - - **Fixed `neural_train` persistence**: Patterns now properly persist to memory instead of being discarded
v2\CHANGELOG.md:907 - - Operations: 2 memory store operations per training (pattern + stats)
v2\CHANGELOG.md:922 - - Added: 4 reasoning agents (PatternMatcher, ContextSynthesizer, MemoryOptimizer, ExperienceCurator)
v2\CHANGELOG.md:972 - **Quantization**: 3 types with 4-32x memory reduction
v2\CHANGELOG.md:1032 - - 🧠 AI & Memory (3): agentdb-memory-patterns, agentdb-vector-search, reasoningbank-intelligence
v2\CHANGELOG.md:1042 - - **Progressive Disclosure**: Skills only load when relevant to task (efficient memory usage)
v2\CHANGELOG.md:1066 - - Deleted: analysis, flow-nexus, github, hive-mind, hooks, memory, pair, sparc, stream-chain, swarm, truth, verify commands

## v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:2 - name: "AgentDB Memory Patterns"
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:3 - description: "Implement persistent memory patterns for AI agents using AgentDB. Includes session memory, long-term storage, pattern learning, and context management. Use when building stateful agents, chat systems, or intelligent assistants."
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:10 - Provides memory management patterns for AI agents using AgentDB's persistent storage and ReasoningBank integration. Enables agents to remember conversations, learn from interactions, and maintain context across sessions.
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:76 - cacheSize: 1000,            // In-memory cache
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:112 - class SessionMemory {
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:256 - optimizeMemory: true,
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:296 - 1. **Enable quantization**: Use scalar/binary for 4-32x memory reduction
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:305 - ### Issue: Memory growing too large
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-memory-patterns\SKILL.md:331 - - **Memory Efficiency**: 4-32x reduction with quantization

## v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-optimization\SKILL.md
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-optimization\SKILL.md:3 - description: "Optimize AgentDB performance with quantization (4-32x memory reduction), HNSW indexing (150x faster search), caching, and batch operations. Use when optimizing memory usage, improving search speed, or scaling to millions of vectors."
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-optimization\SKILL.md:34 - # ✅ Memory Efficiency: 4-32x reduction with quantization
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-optimization\SKILL.md:45 - quantizationType: 'binary',   // 32x memory reduction
v2\.claude\skills\agentdb-optimization\SKILL.md:46 - cacheSize: 1000,               // In-memory cache

## v2\.claude\skills\hooks-automation\SKILL.md
v2\.claude\skills\hooks-automation\SKILL.md:3 - description: Automated coordination, formatting, and learning from Claude Code operations using intelligent hooks with MCP integration. Includes pre/post task hooks, session management, Git integration, memory coordination, and neural pattern training for enhanced development workflows.
v2\.claude\skills\hooks-automation\SKILL.md:12 - This skill provides a comprehensive hook system that automatically manages development operations, coordinates swarm agents, maintains session state, and continuously learns from coding patterns. ## v2\benchmark\archive\real_benchmark_results.json
v2\benchmark\archive\real_benchmark_results.json:24 - "stdout": "\n\ud83e\udde0 Claude-Flow v1.0.70 - Advanced AI Agent Orchestration System\n\nUSAGE:\n  claude-flow <command> [options]\n\nINSTALLATION & SETUP:\n  npx claude-flow@latest init --sparc  # Initialize SPARC development environment\n  \n  The --sparc flag creates:\n  \u2022 .roomodes file with 17 pre-configured SPARC modes\n  \u2022 CLAUDE.md for project instructions\n  \u2022 Ready-to-use TDD and code generation environment\n\nKEY COMMANDS:\n  init [--sparc]                       Initialize project with Claude integration\n  start [--ui]                         Start orchestration (--ui for enhanced UI)\n  spawn <type> [--name <name>]         Create AI agent (alias for agent spawn)\n  agent spawn <type> [--name <name>]   Create AI agent (researcher, coder, analyst)\n  sparc <subcommand>                   SPARC-based development modes\n  memory <subcommand>                  Manage persistent memory\n  status                               Show system status\n\nCOMMAND CATEGORIES:\n  Core:         init, start, status, config\n  Agents:       agent, task, claude\n  Development:  sparc, memory, workflow\n  Infrastructure: mcp, terminal, session\n  Enterprise:   project, deploy, cloud, security, analytics\n\nQUICK START:\n  npx -y claude-flow@latest init --sparc # First-time setup with SPARC modes\n  ./claude-flow start --ui              # Interactive process management UI\n  ./claude-flow sparc modes             # List available development modes\n  ./claude-flow sparc \"build app\"       # Run SPARC orchestrator (default)\n  ./claude-flow sparc run code \"feature\" # Run specific mode (auto-coder)\n  ./claude-flow sparc tdd \"tests\"       # Run test-driven development\n  ./claude-flow memory store key \"data\"  # Store information\n  ./claude-flow status                  # Check system status\n\nGET DETAILED HELP:\n  claude-flow help <command>           # Show command-specific help\n  claude-flow <command> --help         # Alternative help syntax\n  \n  Examples:\n    claude-flow help sparc             # SPARC development commands\n    claude-flow help agent             # Agent management commands\n    claude-flow help memory            # Memory operations\n    claude-flow agent --help           # Agent subcommands\n\nCOMMON OPTIONS:\n  --verbose, -v                        Enable detailed output\n  --help                               Show command help\n  --config <path>                      Use custom config file\n\nDocumentation: https://github.com/ruvnet/claude-code-flow\n\nCreated by rUv - Built with \u2764\ufe0f for the Claude community\n\n\nRegistered Commands:\n  start       Start the orchestration system\n  agent       Manage agents (spawn, list, terminate, info)\n  task        Comprehensive task management with orchestration features\n  config      Manage configuration (show, get, set, init, validate)\n  status      Show system status\n  memory      Manage memory (query, export, import, stats, cleanup)\n  monitor     Monitor system in real-time\n  ui          UI and terminal compatibility tools\n  mcp         Manage MCP server and tools\n  session     Manage terminal sessions\n  mcp         Manage MCP server and tools\n  workflow    Execute workflow files\n  repl        Start interactive REPL mode\n  init        Initialize Claude-Flow project\n  spawn       Spawn a new agent (alias for agent spawn)\n  sparc       SPARC-based development commands with comprehensive mode support\n  claude      Claude API integration with conversation management and context optimization\n  project     Project management (Enterprise feature)\n  deploy      Deployment operations (Enterprise feature)\n  cloud       Cloud infrastructure management (Enterprise feature)\n  security    Security and compliance tools (Enterprise feature)\n  analytics   Analytics and insights (Enterprise feature)\n  swarm       Swarm-based AI agent coordination\n  help        Show help for a specific command\n\nUse \"claude-flow help <command>\" for detailed usage information\n",

## v2\benchmark\archive\old-files\real_benchmark_results.json
v2\benchmark\archive\old-files\real_benchmark_results.json:24 - "stdout": "\n\ud83e\udde0 Claude-Flow v1.0.70 - Advanced AI Agent Orchestration System\n\nUSAGE:\n  claude-flow <command> [options]\n\nINSTALLATION & SETUP:\n  npx claude-flow@latest init --sparc  # Initialize SPARC development environment\n  \n  The --sparc flag creates:\n  \u2022 .roomodes file with 17 pre-configured SPARC modes\n  \u2022 CLAUDE.md for project instructions\n  \u2022 Ready-to-use TDD and code generation environment\n\nKEY COMMANDS:\n  init [--sparc]                       Initialize project with Claude integration\n  start [--ui]                         Start orchestration (--ui for enhanced UI)\n  spawn <type> [--name <name>]         Create AI agent (alias for agent spawn)\n  agent spawn <type> [--name <name>]   Create AI agent (researcher, coder, analyst)\n  sparc <subcommand>                   SPARC-based development modes\n  memory <subcommand>                  Manage persistent memory\n  status                               Show system status\n\nCOMMAND CATEGORIES:\n  Core:         init, start, status, config\n  Agents:       agent, task, claude\n  Development:  sparc, memory, workflow\n  Infrastructure: mcp, terminal, session\n  Enterprise:   project, deploy, cloud, security, analytics\n\nQUICK START:\n  npx -y claude-flow@latest init --sparc # First-time setup with SPARC modes\n  ./claude-flow start --ui              # Interactive process management UI\n  ./claude-flow sparc modes             # List available development modes\n  ./claude-flow sparc \"build app\"       # Run SPARC orchestrator (default)\n  ./claude-flow sparc run code \"feature\" # Run specific mode (auto-coder)\n  ./claude-flow sparc tdd \"tests\"       # Run test-driven development\n  ./claude-flow memory store key \"data\"  # Store information\n  ./claude-flow status                  # Check system status\n\nGET DETAILED HELP:\n  claude-flow help <command>           # Show command-specific help\n  claude-flow <command> --help         # Alternative help syntax\n  \n  Examples:\n    claude-flow help sparc             # SPARC development commands\n    claude-flow help agent             # Agent management commands\n    claude-flow help memory            # Memory operations\n    claude-flow agent --help           # Agent subcommands\n\nCOMMON OPTIONS:\n  --verbose, -v                        Enable detailed output\n  --help                               Show command help\n  --config <path>                      Use custom config file\n\nDocumentation: https://github.com/ruvnet/claude-code-flow\n\nCreated by rUv - Built with \u2764\ufe0f for the Claude community\n\n\nRegistered Commands:\n  start       Start the orchestration system\n  agent       Manage agents (spawn, list, terminate, info)\n  task        Comprehensive task management with orchestration features\n  config      Manage configuration (show, get, set, init, validate)\n  status      Show system status\n  memory      Manage memory (query, export, import, stats, cleanup)\n  monitor     Monitor system in real-time\n  ui          UI and terminal compatibility tools\n  mcp         Manage MCP server and tools\n  session     Manage terminal sessions\n  mcp         Manage MCP server and tools\n  workflow    Execute workflow files\n  repl        Start interactive REPL mode\n  init        Initialize Claude-Flow project\n  spawn       Spawn a new agent (alias for agent spawn)\n  sparc       SPARC-based development commands with comprehensive mode support\n  claude      Claude API integration with conversation management and context optimization\n  project     Project management (Enterprise feature)\n  deploy      Deployment operations (Enterprise feature)\n  cloud       Cloud infrastructure management (Enterprise feature)\n  security    Security and compliance tools (Enterprise feature)\n  analytics   Analytics and insights (Enterprise feature)\n  swarm       Swarm-based AI agent coordination\n  help        Show help for a specific command\n\nUse \"claude-flow help <command>\" for detailed usage information\n",

    1\u2192# Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     4\u2192Functions to determine if a model is separable, i.e.\\n     5\u2192if the model outputs are independent.\\n     6\u2192\\n     7\u2192It analyzes ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` and the operators\\n     8\u2192in a compound model by stepping through the transforms\\n     9\u2192and creating a ``coord_matrix`` of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192Each modeling operator is represented by a function which\\n    13\u2192takes two simple models (or two ``coord_matrix`` arrays) and\\n    14\u2192returns an array of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192import numpy as np\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192from .core import CompoundModel, Model, ModelDefinitionError\\n    21\u2192from .mappings import Mapping\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192__all__ = [\\\"is_separable\\\", \\\"separability_matrix\\\"]\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192def is_separable(transform):\\n    27\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    28\u2192    A separability test for the outputs of a transform.\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192    Parameters\\n    31\u2192    ----------\\n    32\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    33\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192    Returns\\n    36\u2192    -------\\n    37\u2192    is_separable : ndarray\\n    38\u2192        A boolean array with size ``transform.n_outputs`` where\\n    39\u2192        each element indicates whether the output is independent\\n    40\u2192        and the result of a separable transform.\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192    Examples\\n    43\u2192    --------\\n    44\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    45\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    46\u2192        array([ True,  True]...)\\n    47\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    48\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    49\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    50\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    51\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    52\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    53\u2192        array([ True,  True,  True,  True]...)\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    56\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    57\u2192        is_separable = np.array([False] * transform.n_outputs).T\\n    58\u2192        return is_separable\\n    59\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n    60\u2192    is_separable = separable_matrix.sum(1)\\n    61\u2192    is_separable = np.where(is_separable != 1, False, True)\\n    62\u2192    return is_separable\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192def separability_matrix(transform):\\n    66\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    67\u2192    Compute the correlation between outputs and inputs.\\n    68\u2192\\n    69\u2192    Parameters\\n    70\u2192    ----------\\n    71\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    72\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    73\u2192\\n    74\u2192    Returns\\n    75\u2192    -------\\n    76\u2192    separable_matrix : ndarray\\n    77\u2192        A boolean correlation matrix of shape (n_outputs, n_inputs).\\n    78\u2192        Indicates the dependence of outputs on inputs. For completely\\n    79\u2192        independent outputs, the diagonal elements are True and\\n    80\u2192        off-diagonal elements are False.\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192    Examples\\n    83\u2192    --------\\n    84\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    85\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    86\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    87\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    88\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    89\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    90\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    91\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    92\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    93\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True], [ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    96\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    97\u2192        return np.ones((transform.n_outputs, transform.n_inputs), dtype=np.bool_)\\n    98\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n    99\u2192    separable_matrix = np.where(separable_matrix != 0, True, False)\\n   100\u2192    return separable_matrix\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192def _compute_n_outputs(left, right):\\n   104\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   105\u2192    Compute the number of outputs of two models.\\n   106\u2192\\n   107\u2192    The two models are the left and right model to an operation in\\n   108\u2192    the expression tree of a compound model.\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192    Parameters\\n   111\u2192    ----------\\n   112\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   113\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   114\u2192\\n   115\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   116\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   117\u2192        lnout = left.n_outputs\\n   118\u2192    else:\\n   119\u2192        lnout = left.shape[0]\\n   120\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   121\u2192        rnout = right.n_outputs\\n   122\u2192    else:\\n   123\u2192        rnout = right.shape[0]\\n   124\u2192    noutp = lnout + rnout\\n   125\u2192    return noutp\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192def _arith_oper(left, right):\\n   129\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   130\u2192    Function corresponding to one of the arithmetic operators\\n   131\u2192    ['+', '-'. '*', '/', '**'].\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192    This always returns a nonseparable output.\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192    Parameters\\n   136\u2192    ----------\\n   137\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   138\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192    Returns\\n   141\u2192    -------\\n   142\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   143\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   144\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input):\\n   147\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   148\u2192            # Models have the same number of inputs and outputs.\\n   149\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.n_outputs, input.n_inputs\\n   150\u2192        else:\\n   151\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.shape\\n   152\u2192        return n_inputs, n_outputs\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192    left_inputs, left_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(left)\\n   155\u2192    right_inputs, right_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(right)\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192    if left_inputs != right_inputs or left_outputs != right_outputs:\\n   158\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   159\u2192            \\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: left\\\"\\n   160\u2192            f\\\" (n_inputs={left_inputs}, n_outputs={left_outputs}) and right\\\"\\n   161\u2192            f\\\" (n_inputs={right_inputs}, n_outputs={right_outputs}); models must have\\\"\\n   162\u2192            \\\" the same n_inputs and the same n_outputs for this operator.\\\"\\n   163\u2192        )\\n   164\u2192\\n   165\u2192    result = np.ones((left_outputs, left_inputs))\\n   166\u2192    return result\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192def _coord_matrix(model, pos, noutp):\\n   170\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   171\u2192    Create an array representing inputs and outputs of a simple model.\\n   172\u2192\\n   173\u2192    The array has a shape (noutp, model.n_inputs).\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192    Parameters\\n   176\u2192    ----------\\n   177\u2192    model : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   178\u2192        model\\n   179\u2192    pos : str\\n   180\u2192        Position of this model in the expression tree.\\n   181\u2192        One of ['left', 'right'].\\n   182\u2192    noutp : int\\n   183\u2192        Number of outputs of the compound model of which the input model\\n   184\u2192        is a left or right child.\\n   185\u2192\\n   186\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   187\u2192    if isinstance(model, Mapping):\\n   188\u2192        axes = []\\n   189\u2192        for i in model.mapping:\\n   190\u2192            axis = np.zeros((model.n_inputs,))\\n   191\u2192            axis[i] = 1\\n   192\u2192            axes.append(axis)\\n   193\u2192        m = np.vstack(axes)\\n   194\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   195\u2192        if pos == \\\"left\\\":\\n   196\u2192            mat[: model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = m\\n   197\u2192        else:\\n   198\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs :, -model.n_inputs :] = m\\n   199\u2192        return mat\\n   200\u2192    if not model.separable:\\n   201\u2192        # this does not work for more than 2 coordinates\\n   202\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   203\u2192        if pos == \\\"left\\\":\\n   204\u2192            mat[: model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = 1\\n   205\u2192        else:\\n   206\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs :, -model.n_inputs :] = 1\\n   207\u2192    else:\\n   208\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   209\u2192\\n   210\u2192        for i in range(model.n_inputs):\\n   211\u2192            mat[i, i] = 1\\n   212\u2192        if pos == \\\"right\\\":\\n   213\u2192            mat = np.roll(mat, (noutp - model.n_outputs))\\n   214\u2192    return mat\\n   215\u2192\\n   216\u2192\\n   217\u2192def _cstack(left, right):\\n   218\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   219\u2192    Function corresponding to '&' operation.\\n   220\u2192\\n   221\u2192    Parameters\\n   222\u2192    ----------\\n   223\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   224\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   225\u2192\\n   226\u2192    Returns\\n   227\u2192    -------\\n   228\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   229\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   230\u2192\\n   231\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   232\u2192    noutp = _compute_n_outputs(left, right)\\n   233\u2192\\n   234\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   235\u2192        cleft = _coord_matrix(left, \\\"left\\\", noutp)\\n   236\u2192    else:\\n   237\u2192        cleft = np.zeros((noutp, left.shape[1]))\\n   238\u2192        cleft[: left.shape[0], : left.shape[1]] = left\\n   239\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   240\u2192        cright = _coord_matrix(right, \\\"right\\\", noutp)\\n   241\u2192    else:\\n   242\u2192        cright = np.zeros((noutp, right.shape[1]))\\n   243\u2192        cright[-right.shape[0] :, -right.shape[1] :] = right\\n   244\u2192\\n   245\u2192    return np.hstack([cleft, cright])\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192\\n   248\u2192def _cdot(left, right):\\n   249\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   250\u2192    Function corresponding to \\\"|\\\" operation.\\n   251\u2192\\n   252\u2192    Parameters\\n   253\u2192    ----------\\n   254\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   255\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   256\u2192\\n   257\u2192    Returns\\n   258\u2192    -------\\n   259\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   260\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   261\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   262\u2192    left, right = right, left\\n   263\u2192\\n   264\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input, position):\\n   265\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   266\u2192        Return ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` for a model or coord_matrix.\\n   267\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   268\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   269\u2192            # Check if it's a CompoundModel and handle it differently\\n   270\u2192            if isinstance(input, CompoundModel):\\n   271\u2192                # For CompoundModels, use _separable to get the separability matrix\\n   272\u2192                coords = _separable(input)\\n   273\u2192            else:\\n   274\u2192                # For simple Models, use _coord_matrix as before\\n   275\u2192                coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n   276\u2192        else:\\n   277\u2192            coords = input\\n   278\u2192        return coords\\n   279\u2192\\n   280\u2192    cleft = _n_inputs_outputs(left, \\\"left\\\")\\n   281\u2192    cright = _n_inputs_outputs(right, \\\"right\\\")\\n   282\u2192\\n   283\u2192    try:\\n   284\u2192        result = np.dot(cleft, cright)\\n   285\u2192    except ValueError:\\n   286\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   287\u2192            'Models cannot be combined with the \\\"|\\\" operator; '\\n   288\u2192            f\\\"left coord_matrix is {cright}, right coord_matrix is {cleft}\\\"\\n   289\u2192        )\\n   290\u2192    return result\\n   291\u2192\\n   292\u2192\\n   293\u2192def _separable(transform):\\n   294\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   295\u2192    Calculate the separability of outputs.\\n   296\u2192\\n   297\u2192    Parameters\\n   298\u2192    ----------\\n   299\u2192    transform : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   300\u2192        A transform (usually a compound model).\\n   301\u2192\\n   302\u2192    Returns :\\n   303\u2192    is_separable : ndarray of dtype np.bool\\n   304\u2192        An array of shape (transform.n_outputs,) of boolean type\\n   305\u2192        Each element represents the separablity of the corresponding output.\\n   306\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   307\u2192    if (\\n   308\u2192        transform_matrix := transform._calculate_separability_matrix()\\n   309\u2192    ) is not NotImplemented:\\n   310\u2192        return transform_matrix\\n   311\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n   312\u2192        sepleft = _separable(transform.left)\\n   313\u2192        sepright = _separable(transform.right)\\n   314\u2192        return _operators[transform.op](sepleft, sepright)\\n   315\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, Model):\\n   316\u2192        return _coord_matrix(transform, \\\"left\\\", transform.n_outputs)\\n   317\u2192\\n   318\u2192\\n   319\u2192# Maps modeling operators to a function computing and represents the\\n   320\u2192# relationship of axes as an array of 0-es and 1-s\\n   321\u2192_operators = {\\n   322\u2192    \\\"&\\\": _cstack,\\n   323\u2192    \\\"|\\\": _cdot,\\n   324\u2192    \\\"+\\\": _arith_oper,\\n   325\u2192    \\\"-\\\": _arith_oper,\\n   326\u2192    \\\"*\\\": _arith_oper,\\n   327\u2192    \\\"/\\\": _arith_oper,\\n   328\u2192    \\\"**\\\": _arith_oper,\\n   329\u2192}\\n   330\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a python3\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nDebug script for astropy separability_matrix issue with nested CompoundModels.\\nTesting the failure case and analyzing the computation step by step.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nimport numpy as np\\nfrom astropy.modeling import models as m\\nfrom astropy.modeling.separable import separability_matrix\\nfrom astropy.modeling.core import CompoundModel\\n\\ndef test_nested_compound_model():\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Test the specific failing case with nested CompoundModels.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"=== Testing Nested CompoundModel Separability ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Create the nested model that fails\\n    cm = ((m.Shift(1) & m.Shift(2)) | m.Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1])) * (m.Polynomial1D(1) & m.Polynomial1D(2))\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Compound Model: {cm}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Model inputs: {cm.n_inputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Model outputs: {cm.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Analyze the model structure\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Model Structure Analysis ===\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Left side: {cm.left}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Left n_inputs: {cm.left.n_inputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Left n_outputs: {cm.left.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Right side: {cm.right}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Right n_inputs: {cm.right.n_inputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Right n_outputs: {cm.right.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Try to compute separability matrix\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Computing Separability Matrix ===\\\")\\n    try:\\n        result = separability_matrix(cm)\\n        print(f\\\"Result shape: {result.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"Result:\\\\n{result}\\\")\\n        \\n        # Expected should be 4x2 matrix\\n        print(f\\\"Expected shape: (4, 2)\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"Shape matches expected: {result.shape == (4, 2)}\\\")\\n        \\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"ERROR: {type(e).__name__}: {e}\\\")\\n        import traceback\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\n    \\n    return cm\\n\\ndef analyze_subcomponents():\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Analyze each subcomponent separately.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Analyzing Subcomponents ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Test individual components\\n    shift1 = m.Shift(1)\\n    shift2 = m.Shift(2)\\n    mapping = m.Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1])\\n    poly1 = m.Polynomial1D(1)\\n    poly2 = m.Polynomial1D(2)\\n    \\n    components = [\\n        (\\\"Shift(1)\\\", shift1),\\n        (\\\"Shift(2)\\\", shift2),\\n        (\\\"Mapping([0,1,0,1])\\\", mapping),\\n        (\\\"Polynomial1D(1)\\\", poly1),\\n        (\\\"Polynomial1D(2)\\\", poly2)\\n    ]\\n    \\n    for name, comp in components:\\n        try:\\n            sep_mat = separability_matrix(comp)\\n            print(f\\\"{name}: {comp.n_inputs}\u2192{comp.n_outputs}, matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            print(f\\\"{name}: ERROR - {e}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Test intermediate compound models\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Testing Intermediate Compounds ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Shift(1) & Shift(2)\\n    shifts_combined = shift1 & shift2\\n    print(f\\\"Shift(1) & Shift(2): {shifts_combined.n_inputs}\u2192{shifts_combined.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    try:\\n        sep_mat = separability_matrix(shifts_combined)\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n    \\n    # (Shift(1) & Shift(2)) | Mapping\\n    left_side = shifts_combined | mapping\\n    print(f\\\"Left side: {left_side.n_inputs}\u2192{left_side.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    try:\\n        sep_mat = separability_matrix(left_side)\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Polynomial1D(1) & Polynomial1D(2)\\n    polys_combined = poly1 & poly2\\n    print(f\\\"Poly1D(1) & Poly1D(2): {polys_combined.n_inputs}\u2192{polys_combined.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    try:\\n        sep_mat = separability_matrix(polys_combined)\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n\\ndef debug_separability_computation(model):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Debug the separability matrix computation step by step.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Debugging Separability Computation ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Import the internal functions to trace execution\\n    try:\\n        from astropy.modeling.separable import _separable, _compute_n_outputs\\n        \\n        print(f\\\"Model type: {type(model)}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"Is CompoundModel: {isinstance(model, CompoundModel)}\\\")\\n        \\n        if isinstance(model, CompoundModel):\\n            print(f\\\"Operator: {model.op}\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"Left: {model.left} ({type(model.left)})\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"Right: {model.right} ({type(model.right)})\\\")\\n            \\n            # Try to compute separability for each side\\n            print(\\\"\\\\n--- Left side separability ---\\\")\\n            try:\\n                left_sep = separability_matrix(model.left)\\n                print(f\\\"Left separability shape: {left_sep.shape}\\\")\\n                print(f\\\"Left separability:\\\\n{left_sep}\\\")\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Left separability ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            print(\\\"\\\\n--- Right side separability ---\\\")\\n            try:\\n                right_sep = separability_matrix(model.right)\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability shape: {right_sep.shape}\\\")\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability:\\\\n{right_sep}\\\")\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n        \\n    except ImportError as e:\\n        print(f\\\"Cannot import internal functions: {e}\\\")\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    # Run the analysis\\n    model = test_nested_compound_model()\\n    analyze_subcomponents()\\n is_separable,\\n    22\u2192    separability_matrix,\\n    23\u2192)\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192sh1 = models.Shift(1, name=\\\"shift1\\\")\\n    26\u2192sh2 = models.Shift(2, name=\\\"sh2\\\")\\n 0, 1), name=\\\"map2\\\")\\n    31\u2192map3 = Mapping((0, 0), name=\\\"map3\\\")\\n    32\u2192rot = models.Rotation2D(2, name=\\\"rotation\\\")\\n    33\u2192p2 = models.Polynomial2D(1, name=\\\"p2\\\")\\n    34\u2192p22 = models.Polynomial2D(2, name=\\\"p22\\\")\\n    35\u2192p1 = models.Polynomial1D(1, name=\\\"p1\\\")\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192cm_4d_expected = (\\n    39\u2192    np.array([False, False, True, True]),\\n    40\u2192    np.array(\\n    41\u2192        [\\n    42\u2192            [True, True, False, False],\\n    43\u2192            [True, True, False, False],\\n    44\u2192            [False, False, True, False],\\n    45\u2192            [False, False, False, True],\\n    46\u2192        ]\\n    47\u2192    ),\\n    48\u2192)\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192compound_models = {\\n    52\u2192    \\\"cm1\\\": (\\n    53\u2192        map3 & sh1 | rot & sh1 | sh1 & sh2 & sh1,\\n    54\u2192        (\\n    55\u2192            np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    56\u2192            np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]),\\n    57\u2192        ),\\n    58\u2192    ),\\n    59\u2192    \\\"cm2\\\": (\\n    60\u2192        sh1 & sh2 | rot | map1 | p2 & p22,\\n    61\u2192        (np.array([False, False]), np.array([[True, True], [True, True]])),\\n    62\u2192    ),\\n    63\u2192    \\\"cm3\\\": (\\n    64\u2192        map2 | rot & scl1,\\n    65\u2192        (\\n    66\u2192            np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    67\u2192            np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]),\\n    68\u2192        ),\\n    69\u2192    ),\\n    70\u2192    \\\"cm4\\\": (\\n    71\u2192        sh1 & sh2 | map2 | rot & scl1,\\n    72\u2192        (\\n    73\u2192            np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    74\u2192            np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]),\\n    75\u2192        ),\\n    76\u2192    ),\\n    77\u2192    \\\"cm5\\\": (\\n    78\u2192        map3 | sh1 & sh2 | scl1 & scl2,\\n    79\u2192        (np.array([False, False]), np.array([[True], [True]])),\\n    80\u2192    ),\\n    81\u2192    \\\"cm7\\\": (\\n    82\u2192        map2 | p2 & sh1,\\n    83\u2192        (np.array([False, True]), np.array([[True, False], [False, True]])),\\n    84\u2192    ),\\n    85\u2192    \\\"cm8\\\": (rot & (sh1 & sh2), cm_4d_expected),\\n    86\u2192    \\\"cm9\\\": (rot & sh1 & sh2, cm_4d_expected),\\n    87\u2192    \\\"cm10\\\": ((rot & sh1) & sh2, cm_4d_expected),\\n    88\u2192    \\\"cm11\\\": (\\n    89\u2192        rot & sh1 & (scl1 & scl2),\\n    90\u2192        (\\n    91\u2192            np.array([False, False, True, True, True]),\\n    92\u2192            np.array(\\n    93\u2192                [\\n    94\u2192                    [True, True, False, False, False],\\n    95\u2192                    [True, True, False, False, False],\\n    96\u2192                    [False, False, True, False, False],\\n    97\u2192                    [False, False, False, True, False],\\n    98\u2192                    [False, False, False, False, True],\\n    99\u2192                ]\\n   100\u2192            ),\\n   101\u2192        ),\\n   102\u2192    ),\\n   103\u2192}\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192def test_coord_matrix():\\n   107\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p2, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   108\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1, 1], [0, 0]]), c)\\n   109\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p2, \\\"right\\\", 2)\\n   110\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0, 0], [1, 1]]), c)\\n   111\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p1, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   112\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [0]]), c)\\n   113\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p1, \\\"left\\\", 1)\\n   114\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1]]), c)\\n   115\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   116\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [0]]), c)\\n   117\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, \\\"right\\\", 2)\\n   118\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0], [1]]), c)\\n   119\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, \\\"right\\\", 3)\\n   120\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0], [0], [1]]), c)\\n   121\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(map3, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   122\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [1]]), c)\\n   123\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(map3, \\\"left\\\", 3)\\n   124\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [1], [0]]), c)\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192def test_cdot():\\n   128\u2192    result = _cdot(sh1, scl1)\\n   129\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1]]))\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192    result = _cdot(rot, p2)\\n   132\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2, 2]]))\\n   133\u2192\\n   134\u2192    result = _cdot(rot, rot)\\n   135\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2, 2], [2, 2]]))\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192    result = _cdot(Mapping((0, 0)), rot)\\n   138\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2], [2]]))\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192    with pytest.raises(\\n   141\u2192        ModelDefinitionError,\\n   142\u2192        match=r\\\"Models cannot be combined with the \\\\\\\"|\\\\\\\" operator; .*\\\",\\n   143\u2192    ):\\n   144\u2192        _cdot(sh1, map1)\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\n   147\u2192def test_cstack():\\n   148\u2192    result = _cstack(sh1, scl1)\\n   149\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 0], [0, 1]]))\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192    result = _cstack(sh1, rot)\\n   152\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 0, 0], [0, 1, 1], [0, 1, 1]]))\\n   153\u2192    result = _cstack(rot, sh1)\\n   154\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1, 0], [1, 1, 0], [0, 0, 1]]))\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192def test_arith_oper():\\n   158\u2192    # Models as inputs\\n   159\u2192    result = _arith_oper(sh1, scl1)\\n   160\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1]]))\\n   161\u2192    result = _arith_oper(rot, rot)\\n   162\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1], [1, 1]]))\\n   163\u2192\\n   164\u2192    # ndarray\\n   165\u2192    result = _arith_oper(np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]), np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]))\\n   166\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1], [1, 1]]))\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192    # Error\\n   169\u2192    with pytest.raises(\\n   170\u2192        ModelDefinitionError, match=r\\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: .*\\\"\\n   171\u2192    ):\\n   172\u2192        _arith_oper(sh1, map1)\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192@pytest.mark.parametrize((\\\"compound_model\\\", \\\"result\\\"), compound_models.values())\\n   176\u2192def test_separable(compound_model, result):\\n   177\u2192    assert_allclose(is_separable(compound_model), result[0])\\n   178\u2192    assert_allclose(separability_matrix(compound_model), result[1])\\n   179\u2192\\n   180\u2192\\n   181\u2192def test_custom_model_separable():\\n   182\u2192    @custom_model\\n   183\u2192    def model_a(x):\\n   184\u2192        return x\\n   185\u2192\\n   186\u2192    assert model_a().separable\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192    @custom_model\\n   189\u2192    def model_c(x, y):\\n   190\u2192        return x + y\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192    assert not model_c().separable\\n   193\u2192    assert np.all(separability_matrix(model_c()) == [True, True])\\n   194\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious.     1\u2192# Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     4\u2192Functions to determine if a model is separable, i.e.\\n     5\u2192if the model outputs are independent.\\n     6\u2192\\n     7\u2192It analyzes ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` and the operators\\n     8\u2192in a compound model by stepping through the transforms\\n     9\u2192and creating a ``coord_matrix`` of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192Each modeling operator is represented by a function which\\n    13\u2192takes two simple models (or two ``coord_matrix`` arrays) and\\n    14\u2192returns an array of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192import numpy as np\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192from .core import Model, ModelDefinitionError, CompoundModel\\n    21\u2192from .mappings import Mapping\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192__all__ = [\\\"is_separable\\\", \\\"separability_matrix\\\"]\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192def is_separable(transform):\\n    28\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    29\u2192    A separability test for the outputs of a transform.\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192    Parameters\\n    32\u2192    ----------\\n    33\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    34\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192    Returns\\n    37\u2192    -------\\n    38\u2192    is_separable : ndarray\\n    39\u2192        A boolean array with size ``transform.n_outputs`` where\\n    40\u2192        each element indicates whether the output is independent\\n    41\u2192        and the result of a separable transform.\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192    Examples\\n    44\u2192    --------\\n    45\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    46\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    47\u2192        array([ True,  True]...)\\n    48\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    49\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    50\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    51\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    52\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    53\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    54\u2192        array([ True,  True,  True,  True]...)\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    57\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    58\u2192        is_separable = np.array([False] * transform.n_outputs).T\\n    59\u2192        return is_separable\\n    60\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n    61\u2192    is_separable = separable_matrix.sum(1)\\n    62\u2192    is_separable = np.where(is_separable != 1, False, True)\\n    63\u2192    return is_separable\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192def separability_matrix(transform):\\n    67\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    68\u2192    Compute the correlation between outputs and inputs.\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192    Parameters\\n    71\u2192    ----------\\n    72\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    73\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192    Returns\\n    76\u2192    -------\\n    77\u2192    separable_matrix : ndarray\\n    78\u2192        A boolean correlation matrix of shape (n_outputs, n_inputs).\\n    79\u2192        Indicates the dependence of outputs on inputs. For completely\\n    80\u2192        independent outputs, the diagonal elements are True and\\n    81\u2192        off-diagonal elements are False.\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192    Examples\\n    84\u2192    --------\\n    85\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    86\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    87\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    88\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    89\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    90\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    91\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    92\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    93\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    94\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True], [ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    97\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    98\u2192        return np.ones((transform.n_outputs, transform.n_inputs),\\n    99\u2192                       dtype=np.bool_)\\n   100\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n   101\u2192    separable_matrix = np.where(separable_matrix != 0, True, False)\\n   102\u2192    return separable_matrix\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192def _compute_n_outputs(left, right):\\n   106\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   107\u2192    Compute the number of outputs of two models.\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192    The two models are the left and right model to an operation in\\n   110\u2192    the expression tree of a compound model.\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192    Parameters\\n   113\u2192    ----------\\n   114\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   115\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   118\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   119\u2192        lnout = left.n_outputs\\n   120\u2192    else:\\n   121\u2192        lnout = left.shape[0]\\n   122\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   123\u2192        rnout = right.n_outputs\\n   124\u2192    else:\\n   125\u2192        rnout = right.shape[0]\\n   126\u2192    noutp = lnout + rnout\\n   127\u2192    return noutp\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192def _arith_oper(left, right):\\n   131\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   132\u2192    Function corresponding to one of the arithmetic operators\\n   133\u2192    ['+', '-'. '*', '/', '**'].\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192    This always returns a nonseparable output.\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192    Parameters\\n   139\u2192    ----------\\n   140\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   141\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192    Returns\\n   144\u2192    -------\\n   145\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   146\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   147\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   148\u2192    # models have the same number of inputs and outputs\\n   149\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input):\\n   150\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   151\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.n_outputs, input.n_inputs\\n   152\u2192        else:\\n   153\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.shape\\n   154\u2192        return n_inputs, n_outputs\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192    left_inputs, left_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(left)\\n   157\u2192    right_inputs, right_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(right)\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192    if left_inputs != right_inputs or left_outputs != right_outputs:\\n   160\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   161\u2192            \\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: left (n_inputs={}, \\\"\\n   162\u2192            \\\"n_outputs={}) and right (n_inputs={}, n_outputs={}); \\\"\\n   163\u2192            \\\"models must have the same n_inputs and the same \\\"\\n   164\u2192            \\\"n_outputs for this operator.\\\".format(\\n   165\u2192                left_inputs, left_outputs, right_inputs, right_outputs))\\n   166\u2192\\n   167\u2192    result = np.ones((left_outputs, left_inputs))\\n   168\u2192    return result\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\n   171\u2192def _coord_matrix(model, pos, noutp):\\n   172\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   173\u2192    Create an array representing inputs and outputs of a simple model.\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192    The array has a shape (noutp, model.n_inputs).\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192    Parameters\\n   178\u2192    ----------\\n   179\u2192    model : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   180\u2192        model\\n   181\u2192    pos : str\\n   182\u2192        Position of this model in the expression tree.\\n   183\u2192        One of ['left', 'right'].\\n   184\u2192    noutp : int\\n   185\u2192        Number of outputs of the compound model of which the input model\\n   186\u2192        is a left or right child.\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   189\u2192    if isinstance(model, Mapping):\\n   190\u2192        axes = []\\n   191\u2192        for i in model.mapping:\\n   192\u2192            axis = np.zeros((model.n_inputs,))\\n   193\u2192            axis[i] = 1\\n   194\u2192            axes.append(axis)\\n   195\u2192        m = np.vstack(axes)\\n   196\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   197\u2192        if pos == 'left':\\n   198\u2192            mat[: model.n_outputs, :model.n_inputs] = m\\n   199\u2192        else:\\n   200\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = m\\n   201\u2192        return mat\\n   202\u2192    if not model.separable:\\n   203\u2192        # this does not work for more than 2 coordinates\\n   204\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   205\u2192        if pos == 'left':\\n   206\u2192            mat[:model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = 1\\n   207\u2192        else:\\n   208\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = 1\\n   209\u2192    else:\\n   210\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   211\u2192\\n   212\u2192        for i in range(model.n_inputs):\\n   213\u2192            mat[i, i] = 1\\n   214\u2192        if pos == 'right':\\n   215\u2192            mat = np.roll(mat, (noutp - model.n_outputs))\\n   216\u2192    return mat\\n   217\u2192\\n   218\u2192\\n   219\u2192def _cstack(left, right):\\n   220\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   221\u2192    Function corresponding to '&' operation.\\n   222\u2192\\n   223\u2192    Parameters\\n   224\u2192    ----------\\n   225\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   226\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   227\u2192\\n   228\u2192    Returns\\n   229\u2192    -------\\n   230\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   231\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   232\u2192\\n   233\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   234\u2192    noutp = _compute_n_outputs(left, right)\\n   235\u2192\\n   236\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   237\u2192        cleft = _coord_matrix(left, 'left', noutp)\\n   238\u2192    else:\\n   239\u2192        cleft = np.zeros((noutp, left.shape[1]))\\n   240\u2192        cleft[: left.shape[0], : left.shape[1]] = left\\n   241\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   242\u2192        cright = _coord_matrix(right, 'right', noutp)\\n   243\u2192    else:\\n   244\u2192        cright = np.zeros((noutp, right.shape[1]))\\n   245\u2192        cright[-right.shape[0]:, -right.shape[1]:] = 1\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192    return np.hstack([cleft, cright])\\n   248\u2192\\n   249\u2192\\n   250\u2192def _cdot(left, right):\\n   251\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   252\u2192    Function corresponding to \\\"|\\\" operation.\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192    Parameters\\n   255\u2192    ----------\\n   256\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   257\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192    Returns\\n   260\u2192    -------\\n   261\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   262\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   263\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192    left, right = right, left\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input, position):\\n   268\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   269\u2192        Return ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` for a model or coord_matrix.\\n   270\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   271\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   272\u2192            coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n   273\u2192        else:\\n   274\u2192            coords = input\\n   275\u2192        return coords\\n   276\u2192\\n   277\u2192    cleft = _n_inputs_outputs(left, 'left')\\n   278\u2192    cright = _n_inputs_outputs(right, 'right')\\n   279\u2192\\n   280\u2192    try:\\n   281\u2192        result = np.dot(cleft, cright)\\n   282\u2192    except ValueError:\\n   283\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   284\u2192            'Models cannot be combined with the \\\"|\\\" operator; '\\n   285\u2192            'left coord_matrix is {}, right coord_matrix is {}'.format(\\n   286\u2192                cright, cleft))\\n   287\u2192    return result\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192\\n   290\u2192def _separable(transform):\\n   291\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   292\u2192    Calculate the separability of outputs.\\n   293\u2192\\n   294\u2192    Parameters\\n   295\u2192    ----------\\n   296\u2192    transform : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   297\u2192        A transform (usually a compound model).\\n   298\u2192\\n   299\u2192    Returns :\\n   300\u2192    is_separable : ndarray of dtype np.bool\\n   301\u2192        An array of shape (transform.n_outputs,) of boolean type\\n   302\u2192        Each element represents the separablity of the corresponding output.\\n   303\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   304\u2192    if (transform_matrix := transform._calculate_separability_matrix()) is not NotImplemented:\\n   305\u2192        return transform_matrix\\n   306\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n   307\u2192        sepleft = _separable(transform.left)\\n   308\u2192        sepright = _separable(transform.right)\\n   309\u2192        return _operators[transform.op](sepleft, sepright)\\n   310\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, Model):\\n   311\u2192        return _coord_matrix(transform, 'left', transform.n_outputs)\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192\\n   314\u2192# Maps modeling operators to a function computing and represents the\\n   315\u2192# relationship of axes as an array of 0-es and 1-s\\n   316\u2192_operators = {'&': _cstack, '|': _cdot, '+': _arith_oper, '-': _arith_oper,\\n   317\u2192              '*': _arith_oper, '/': _arith_oper, '**': _arith_oper}\\n   318\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. astropy.modeling.core import CompoundModel\\n\\ndef debug_nested_compound_model():\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Debug the nested CompoundModel separability issue.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*60)\\n    print(\\\"DEBUGGING NESTED COMPOUND MODEL SEPARABILITY ISSUE\\\")\\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*60)\\n    \\n    # Create simple models\\n    shift1 = models.Shift(1, name=\\\"shift1\\\")\\n    shift2 = models.Shift(2, name=\\\"shift2\\\")\\n    rotation = models.Rotation2D(2, name=\\\"rotation\\\")\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Simple models:\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift1: {shift1.n_inputs} inputs, {shift1.n_outputs} outputs\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift2: {shift2.n_inputs} inputs, {shift2.n_outputs} outputs\\\") \\n    print(f\\\"  rotation: {rotation.n_inputs} inputs, {rotation.n_outputs} outputs\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Create a simple CompoundModel (this should work)\\n    simple_compound = shift1 & shift2\\n    print(f\\\"Simple compound (shift1 & shift2):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Type: {type(simple_compound)}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Is CompoundModel: {isinstance(simple_compound, CompoundModel)}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {simple_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {simple_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {simple_compound.left} (type: {type(simple_compound.left)})\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {simple_compound.right} (type: {type(simple_compound.right)})\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test separability matrix for simple compound\\n    try:\\n        simple_sep_matrix = separability_matrix(simple_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"  Separability matrix for simple compound:\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"    {simple_sep_matrix}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"    Shape: {simple_sep_matrix.shape}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR with simple compound: {e}\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Create a nested CompoundModel (this might fail)\\n    nested_compound = simple_compound | rotation\\n    print(f\\\"Nested compound (simple_compound | rotation):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Type: {type(nested_compound)}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Is CompoundModel: {isinstance(nested_compound, CompoundModel)}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {nested_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {nested_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {nested_compound.left} (type: {type(nested_compound.left)})\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {nested_compound.right} (type: {type(nested_compound.right)})\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test separability matrix for nested compound\\n    try:\\n        nested_sep_matrix = separability_matrix(nested_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"  Separability matrix for nested compound:\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"    {nested_sep_matrix}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"    Shape: {nested_sep_matrix.shape}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR with nested compound: {e}\\\")\\n        import traceback\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Let's trace through the _separable function for the nested case\\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*60)\\n    print(\\\"TRACING _separable FUNCTION FOR NESTED COMPOUND\\\")\\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*60)\\n    \\n    def trace_separable(transform, depth=0):\\n        indent = \\\"  \\\" * depth\\n        print(f\\\"{indent}trace_separable({transform}, type={type(transform)})\\\")\\n        \\n        if hasattr(transform, '_calculate_separability_matrix'):\\n            matrix = transform._calculate_separability_matrix()\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  _calculate_separability_matrix() -> {matrix}\\\")\\n            if matrix is not NotImplemented:\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  returning custom matrix: {matrix}\\\")\\n                return matrix\\n        \\n        if isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  CompoundModel: op='{transform.op}'\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Left: {transform.left} (type: {type(transform.left)})\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Right: {transform.right} (type: {type(transform.right)})\\\")\\n            \\n            sepleft = trace_separable(transform.left, depth + 1)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  sepleft result: {sepleft}\\\")\\n            \\n            sepright = trace_separable(transform.right, depth + 1)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  sepright result: {sepright}\\\")\\n            \\n            from astropy.modeling.separable import _operators\\n            operator_func = _operators[transform.op]\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Using operator function: {operator_func}\\\")\\n            \\n            try:\\n                result = operator_func(sepleft, sepright)\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  operator result: {result}\\\")\\n                return result\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  ERROR in operator: {e}\\\")\\n                raise\\n        else:\\n            from astropy.modeling.separable import _coord_matrix\\n            result = _coord_matrix(transform, 'left', transform.n_outputs)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  _coord_matrix result: {result}\\\")\\n            return result\\n    \\n    try:\\n        result = trace_separable(nested_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"Final result: {result}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"Final error: {e}\\\")\\n        import traceback\\n model structure to test separability\\nclass Model:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimal Model class for testing\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, n_inputs, n_outputs, name=None, separable=True):\\n        self.n_inputs = n_inputs\\n        self.n_outputs = n_outputs\\n        self.name = name\\n        self.separable = separable\\n        \\n    def _calculate_separability_matrix(self):\\n        return NotImplemented\\n\\nclass CompoundModel(Model):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimal CompoundModel class for testing\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, op, left, right, name=None):\\n        self.op = op\\n        self.left = left\\n        self.right = right\\n        self.name = name\\n        \\n        # Calculate combined inputs/outputs\\n        if op == '&':  # concatenation\\n            self.n_inputs = left.n_inputs + right.n_inputs\\n            self.n_outputs = left.n_outputs + right.n_outputs\\n        elif op == '|':  # composition\\n            self.n_inputs = right.n_inputs  # input of rightmost (first applied)\\n            self.n_outputs = left.n_outputs  # output of leftmost (last applied)\\n        \\n    def _calculate_separability_matrix(self):\\n        return NotImplemented\\n\\nclass Mapping(Model):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimal Mapping class for testing\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, mapping, name=None):\\n        self.mapping = mapping\\n        self.n_inputs = max(mapping) + 1\\n        self.n_outputs = len(mapping)\\n        self.name = name\\n        self.separable = True\\n        \\n    def _calculate_separability_matrix(self):\\n separability_matrix\\n    \\n    # Create test models\\n    shift1 = Model(1, 1, \\\"shift1\\\", separable=True)\\n    shift2 = Model(1, 1, \\\"shift2\\\", separable=True)\\n    rotation = Model(2, 2, \\\"rotation\\\", separable=False)\\n    \\n    print(\\\"Test models:\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift1: {shift1.n_inputs}\u2192{shift1.n_outputs}, separable={shift1.separable}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift2: {shift2.n_inputs}\u2192{shift2.n_outputs}, separable={shift2.separable}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  rotation: {rotation.n_inputs}\u2192{rotation.n_outputs}, separable={rotation.separable}\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test 1: Simple compound model (shift1 & shift2)\\n    simple_compound = CompoundModel('&', shift1, shift2, \\\"simple\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Simple compound (shift1 & shift2):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {simple_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {simple_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {simple_compound.left.name}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {simple_compound.right.name}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Test separability\\n    simple_matrix = _separable(simple_compound)\\n    print(f\\\"  _separable result: {simple_matrix}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Shape: {simple_matrix.shape}\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test 2: Nested compound model (simple_compound | rotation)\\n    nested_compound = CompoundModel('|', simple_compound, rotation, \\\"nested\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Nested compound (simple | rotation):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {nested_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {nested_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {nested_compound.left.name} (type: {type(nested_compound.left)})\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {nested_compound.right.name}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Trace the issue step by step\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nTracing _separable for nested compound:\\\")\\n    \\n    def trace_separable(transform, depth=0):\\n        indent = \\\"  \\\" * depth\\n        print(f\\\"{indent}Processing: {getattr(transform, 'name', 'unnamed')} (type: {type(transform).__name__})\\\")\\n        \\n        if hasattr(transform, '_calculate_separability_matrix'):\\n            matrix = transform._calculate_separability_matrix()\\n            if matrix is not NotImplemented:\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  Custom separability matrix: {matrix}\\\")\\n                return matrix\\n        \\n        if isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  CompoundModel with op: '{transform.op}'\\\")\\n            \\n            # Get left and right separability matrices\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Processing left: {getattr(transform.left, 'name', 'unnamed')}\\\")\\n            sepleft = trace_separable(transform.left, depth + 1)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Left result: {sepleft} (shape: {sepleft.shape})\\\")\\n            \\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Processing right: {getattr(transform.right, 'name', 'unnamed')}\\\")\\n operator\\n            operator_func = _operators[transform.op]\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Applying operator '{transform.op}' using {operator_func.__name__}\\\")\\n            \\n {result.shape})\\\")\\n            return result\\n    \\n    try:\\n        nested_matrix = trace_separable(nested_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"\\\\nFinal separability matrix: separability_matrix(model.right)\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability shape: {right_sep.shape}\\\")\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability:\\\\n{right_sep}\\\")\\n
