python3\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nDebug script for astropy separability_matrix issue with nested CompoundModels.\\nTesting the failure case and analyzing the computation step by step.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nimport numpy as np\\nfrom astropy.modeling import models as m\\nfrom astropy.modeling.separable import separability_matrix\\nfrom astropy.modeling.core import CompoundModel\\n\\ndef test_nested_compound_model():\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Test the specific failing case with nested CompoundModels.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"=== Testing Nested CompoundModel Separability ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Create the nested model that fails\\n    cm = ((m.Shift(1) & m.Shift(2)) | m.Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1])) * (m.Polynomial1D(1) & m.Polynomial1D(2))\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Compound Model: {cm}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Model inputs: {cm.n_inputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Model outputs: {cm.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Analyze the model structure\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Model Structure Analysis ===\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Left side: {cm.left}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Left n_inputs: {cm.left.n_inputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Left n_outputs: {cm.left.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Right side: {cm.right}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Right n_inputs: {cm.right.n_inputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Right n_outputs: {cm.right.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Try to compute separability matrix\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Computing Separability Matrix ===\\\")\\n    try:\\n        result = separability_matrix(cm)\\n        print(f\\\"Result shape: {result.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"Result:\\\\n{result}\\\")\\n        \\n        # Expected should be 4x2 matrix\\n        print(f\\\"Expected shape: (4, 2)\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"Shape matches expected: {result.shape == (4, 2)}\\\")\\n        \\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"ERROR: {type(e).__name__}: {e}\\\")\\n        import traceback\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\n    \\n    return cm\\n\\ndef analyze_subcomponents():\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Analyze each subcomponent separately.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Analyzing Subcomponents ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Test individual components\\n    shift1 = m.Shift(1)\\n    shift2 = m.Shift(2)\\n    mapping = m.Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1])\\n    poly1 = m.Polynomial1D(1)\\n    poly2 = m.Polynomial1D(2)\\n    \\n    components = [\\n        (\\\"Shift(1)\\\", shift1),\\n        (\\\"Shift(2)\\\", shift2),\\n        (\\\"Mapping([0,1,0,1])\\\", mapping),\\n        (\\\"Polynomial1D(1)\\\", poly1),\\n        (\\\"Polynomial1D(2)\\\", poly2)\\n    ]\\n    \\n    for name, comp in components:\\n        try:\\n            sep_mat = separability_matrix(comp)\\n            print(f\\\"{name}: {comp.n_inputs}\u2192{comp.n_outputs}, matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            print(f\\\"{name}: ERROR - {e}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Test intermediate compound models\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Testing Intermediate Compounds ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Shift(1) & Shift(2)\\n    shifts_combined = shift1 & shift2\\n    print(f\\\"Shift(1) & Shift(2): {shifts_combined.n_inputs}\u2192{shifts_combined.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    try:\\n        sep_mat = separability_matrix(shifts_combined)\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n    \\n    # (Shift(1) & Shift(2)) | Mapping\\n    left_side = shifts_combined | mapping\\n    print(f\\\"Left side: {left_side.n_inputs}\u2192{left_side.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    try:\\n        sep_mat = separability_matrix(left_side)\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Polynomial1D(1) & Polynomial1D(2)\\n    polys_combined = poly1 & poly2\\n    print(f\\\"Poly1D(1) & Poly1D(2): {polys_combined.n_inputs}\u2192{polys_combined.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    try:\\n        sep_mat = separability_matrix(polys_combined)\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix shape: {sep_mat.shape}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"  Matrix:\\\\n{sep_mat}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n\\ndef debug_separability_computation(model):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Debug the separability matrix computation step by step.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n=== Debugging Separability Computation ===\\\")\\n    \\n    # Import the internal functions to trace execution\\n    try:\\n        from astropy.modeling.separable import _separable, _compute_n_outputs\\n        \\n        print(f\\\"Model type: {type(model)}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"Is CompoundModel: {isinstance(model, CompoundModel)}\\\")\\n        \\n        if isinstance(model, CompoundModel):\\n            print(f\\\"Operator: {model.op}\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"Left: {model.left} print(\\\"\\\\n--- Left side separability ---\\\")\\n            try:\\n                left_sep = separability_matrix(model.left)\\n                print(f\\\"Left separability shape: {left_sep.shape}\\\")\\n                print(f\\\"Left separability:\\\\n{left_sep}\\\")\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Left separability ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            print(\\\"\\\\n--- Right side separability ---\\\")\\n            try:\\n                right_sep = separability_matrix(model.right)\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability shape: {right_sep.shape}\\\")\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability:\\\\n{right_sep}\\\")\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Right separability ERROR: {e}\\\")\\n        \\n    except ImportError as e:\\n        print(f\\\"Cannot import internal functions: {e}\\\")\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    # Run the analysis\\n    model = test_nested_compound_model()\\n    analyze_subcomponents()\\n name=\\\"p2\\\")\\n    34\u2192p22 = models.Polynomial2D(2, name=\\\"p22\\\")\\n    35\u2192p1 = models.Polynomial1D(1, name=\\\"p1\\\")\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192cm_4d_expected = True, False, False],\\n    44\u2192            [False, False, True, False],\\n    45\u2192            [False, False, False, True],\\n    46\u2192        ]\\n    47\u2192    ),\\n    48\u2192)\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192compound_models = {\\n    52\u2192    \\\"cm1\\\": (\\n    53\u2192        map3 & sh1 | rot & sh1 | sh1 & sh2 & sh1,\\n    54\u2192        (\\n    55\u2192            np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    56\u2192            np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]),\\n    57\u2192        ),\\n    58\u2192    ),\\n    59\u2192    \\\"cm2\\\": (\\n    60\u2192        sh1 & sh2 | rot | map1 | p2 & p22,\\n    61\u2192        (np.array([False, False]), np.array([[True, True], [True, True]])),\\n    62\u2192    ),\\n    63\u2192    \\\"cm3\\\": (\\n    64\u2192        map2 | rot & scl1,\\n    65\u2192        (\\n    66\u2192            np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    67\u2192            np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]),\\n    68\u2192        ),\\n    69\u2192    ),\\n    70\u2192    \\\"cm4\\\": (\\n    71\u2192        sh1 & sh2 | map2 | rot & scl1,\\n    72\u2192        (\\n    73\u2192            np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    74\u2192            np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]),\\n    75\u2192        ),\\n    76\u2192    ),\\n    77\u2192    \\\"cm5\\\": (\\n    78\u2192        map3 | sh1 & sh2 | scl1 & scl2,\\n    79\u2192        (np.array([False, False]), np.array([[True], [True]])),\\n    80\u2192    ),\\n    81\u2192    \\\"cm7\\\": (\\n    82\u2192        map2 | p2 & sh1,\\n    83\u2192        (np.array([False, True]), np.array([[True, False], [False, True]])),\\n    84\u2192    ),\\n    85\u2192    \\\"cm8\\\": (rot & (sh1 & sh2), cm_4d_expected),\\n    86\u2192    \\\"cm9\\\": (rot & sh1 & sh2, cm_4d_expected),\\n    87\u2192    \\\"cm10\\\": ((rot & sh1) & sh2, cm_4d_expected),\\n    88\u2192    \\\"cm11\\\": (\\n    89\u2192        rot & sh1 & (scl1 & scl2),\\n    90\u2192        (\\n    91\u2192            np.array([False, False, True, True, True]),\\n    92\u2192            np.array(\\n    93\u2192                [\\n    94\u2192                    [True, True, False, False, False],\\n    95\u2192                    [True, True, False, False, False],\\n    96\u2192                    [False, False, True, False, False],\\n    97\u2192                    [False, False, False, True, False],\\n    98\u2192                    [False, False, False, False, True],\\n    99\u2192                ]\\n   100\u2192            ),\\n   101\u2192        ),\\n   102\u2192    ),\\n   103\u2192}\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192def test_coord_matrix():\\n   107\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p2, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   108\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1, 1], [0, 0]]), c)\\n   109\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p2, \\\"right\\\", 2)\\n   110\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0, 0], [1, 1]]), c)\\n   111\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p1, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   112\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [0]]), c)\\n   113\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p1, \\\"left\\\", 1)\\n   114\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1]]), c)\\n   115\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   116\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [0]]), c)\\n   117\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, \\\"right\\\", 2)\\n   118\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0], [1]]), c)\\n   119\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, \\\"right\\\", 3)\\n   120\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0], [0], [1]]), c)\\n   121\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(map3, \\\"left\\\", 2)\\n   122\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [1]]), c)\\n   123\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(map3, \\\"left\\\", 3)\\n   124\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [1], [0]]), c)\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192def test_cdot():\\n   128\u2192    result = _cdot(sh1, scl1)\\n   129\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1]]))\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192    result = _cdot(rot, p2)\\n   132\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2, 2]]))\\n   133\u2192\\n   134\u2192    result = _cdot(rot, rot)\\n   135\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2, 2], [2, 2]]))\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192    result = _cdot(Mapping((0, 0)), rot)\\n   138\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2], [2]]))\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192    with pytest.raises(\\n   141\u2192        ModelDefinitionError,\\n   142\u2192        match=r\\\"Models cannot be combined with the \\\\\\\"|\\\\\\\" operator; .*\\\",\\n   143\u2192    ):\\n   144\u2192        _cdot(sh1, map1)\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\n   147\u2192def test_cstack():\\n   148\u2192    result = _cstack(sh1, scl1)\\n   149\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 0], [0, 1]]))\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192    result = _cstack(sh1, rot)\\n   152\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 0, 0], [0, 1, 1], [0, 1, 1]]))\\n   153\u2192    result = _cstack(rot, sh1)\\n   154\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1, 0], [1, 1, 0], [0, 0, 1]]))\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192def test_arith_oper():\\n   158\u2192    # Models as inputs\\n   159\u2192    result = _arith_oper(sh1, scl1)\\n   160\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1]]))\\n   161\u2192    result = _arith_oper(rot, rot)\\n   162\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1], [1, 1]]))\\n   163\u2192\\n   164\u2192    # ndarray\\n   165\u2192    result = _arith_oper(np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]), np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]))\\n   166\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1], [1, 1]]))\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192    # Error\\n   169\u2192    with pytest.raises(\\n   170\u2192        ModelDefinitionError, match=r\\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: .*\\\"\\n   171\u2192    ):\\n   172\u2192        _arith_oper(sh1, map1)\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192@pytest.mark.parametrize((\\\"compound_model\\\", \\\"result\\\"), compound_models.values())\\n   176\u2192def test_separable(compound_model, result):\\n   177\u2192    assert_allclose(is_separable(compound_model), result[0])\\n   178\u2192    assert_allclose(separability_matrix(compound_model), result[1])\\n   179\u2192\\n   180\u2192\\n   181\u2192def test_custom_model_separable():\\n   182\u2192    @custom_model\\n   183\u2192    def model_a(x):\\n   184\u2192        return x\\n   185\u2192\\n   186\u2192    assert model_a().separable\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192    @custom_model\\n   189\u2192    def model_c(x, y):\\n   190\u2192        return x + y\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192    assert not model_c().separable\\n   193\u2192    assert np.all(separability_matrix(model_c()) == [True, True])\\n   194\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious.     1\u2192# Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     4\u2192Functions to determine if a model is separable, i.e.\\n     5\u2192if the model outputs are independent.\\n     6\u2192\\n     7\u2192It analyzes ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` and the operators\\n     8\u2192in a compound model by stepping through the transforms\\n     9\u2192and creating a ``coord_matrix`` of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192Each modeling operator is represented by a function which\\n    13\u2192takes two simple models (or two ``coord_matrix`` arrays) and\\n    14\u2192returns an array of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192import numpy as np\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192from .core import Model, ModelDefinitionError, CompoundModel\\n    21\u2192from .mappings import Mapping\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192__all__ = [\\\"is_separable\\\", \\\"separability_matrix\\\"]\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192def is_separable(transform):\\n    28\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    29\u2192    A separability test for the outputs of a transform.\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192    Parameters\\n    32\u2192    ----------\\n    33\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    34\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192    Returns\\n    37\u2192    -------\\n    38\u2192    is_separable : ndarray\\n    39\u2192        A boolean array with size ``transform.n_outputs`` where\\n    40\u2192        each element indicates whether the output is independent\\n    41\u2192        and the result of a separable transform.\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192    Examples\\n    44\u2192    --------\\n    45\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    46\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    47\u2192        array([ True,  True]...)\\n    48\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    49\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    50\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    51\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    52\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    53\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    54\u2192        array([ True,  True,  True,  True]...)\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    57\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    58\u2192        is_separable = np.array([False] * transform.n_outputs).T\\n    59\u2192        return is_separable\\n    60\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n    61\u2192    is_separable = separable_matrix.sum(1)\\n    62\u2192    is_separable = np.where(is_separable != 1, False, True)\\n    63\u2192    return is_separable\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192def separability_matrix(transform):\\n    67\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    68\u2192    Compute the correlation between outputs and inputs.\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192    Parameters\\n    71\u2192    ----------\\n    72\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    73\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192    Returns\\n    76\u2192    -------\\n    77\u2192    separable_matrix : ndarray\\n    78\u2192        A boolean correlation matrix of shape (n_outputs, n_inputs).\\n    79\u2192        Indicates the dependence of outputs on inputs. For completely\\n    80\u2192        independent outputs, the diagonal elements are True and\\n    81\u2192        off-diagonal elements are False.\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192    Examples\\n    84\u2192    --------\\n    85\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    86\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    87\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    88\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    89\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    90\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    91\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    92\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    93\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    94\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True], [ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    97\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    98\u2192        return np.ones((transform.n_outputs, transform.n_inputs),\\n    99\u2192                       dtype=np.bool_)\\n   100\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n   101\u2192    separable_matrix = np.where(separable_matrix != 0, True, False)\\n   102\u2192    return separable_matrix\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192def _compute_n_outputs(left, right):\\n   106\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   107\u2192    Compute the number of outputs of two models.\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192    The two models are the left and right model to an operation in\\n   110\u2192    the expression tree of a compound model.\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192    Parameters\\n   113\u2192    ----------\\n   114\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   115\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   118\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   119\u2192        lnout = left.n_outputs\\n   120\u2192    else:\\n   121\u2192        lnout = left.shape[0]\\n   122\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   123\u2192        rnout = right.n_outputs\\n   124\u2192    else:\\n   125\u2192        rnout = right.shape[0]\\n   126\u2192    noutp = lnout + rnout\\n   127\u2192    return noutp\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192def _arith_oper(left, right):\\n   131\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   132\u2192    Function corresponding to one of the arithmetic operators\\n   133\u2192    ['+', '-'. '*', '/', '**'].\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192    This always returns a nonseparable output.\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192    Parameters\\n   139\u2192    ----------\\n   140\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   141\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192    Returns\\n   144\u2192    -------\\n   145\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   146\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   147\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   148\u2192    # models have the same number of inputs and outputs\\n   149\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input):\\n   150\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   151\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.n_outputs, input.n_inputs\\n   152\u2192        else:\\n   153\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.shape\\n   154\u2192        return n_inputs, n_outputs\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192    left_inputs, left_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(left)\\n   157\u2192    right_inputs, right_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(right)\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192    if left_inputs != right_inputs or left_outputs != right_outputs:\\n   160\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   161\u2192            \\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: left (n_inputs={}, \\\"\\n   162\u2192            \\\"n_outputs={}) and right (n_inputs={}, n_outputs={}); \\\"\\n   163\u2192            \\\"models must have the same n_inputs and the same \\\"\\n   164\u2192            \\\"n_outputs for this operator.\\\".format(\\n   165\u2192                left_inputs, left_outputs, right_inputs, right_outputs))\\n   166\u2192\\n   167\u2192    result = np.ones((left_outputs, left_inputs))\\n   168\u2192    return result\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\n   171\u2192def _coord_matrix(model, pos, noutp):\\n   172\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   173\u2192    Create an array representing inputs and outputs of a simple model.\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192    The array has a shape (noutp, model.n_inputs).\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192    Parameters\\n   178\u2192    ----------\\n   179\u2192    model : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   180\u2192        model\\n   181\u2192    pos : str\\n   182\u2192        Position of this model in the expression tree.\\n   183\u2192        One of ['left', 'right'].\\n   184\u2192    noutp : int\\n   185\u2192        Number of outputs of the compound model of which the input model\\n   186\u2192        is a left or right child.\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   189\u2192    if isinstance(model, Mapping):\\n   190\u2192        axes = []\\n   191\u2192        for i in model.mapping:\\n   192\u2192            axis = np.zeros((model.n_inputs,))\\n   193\u2192            axis[i] = 1\\n   194\u2192            axes.append(axis)\\n   195\u2192        m = np.vstack(axes)\\n   196\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   197\u2192        if pos == 'left':\\n   198\u2192            mat[: model.n_outputs, :model.n_inputs] = m\\n   199\u2192        else:\\n   200\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = m\\n   201\u2192        return mat\\n   202\u2192    if not model.separable:\\n   203\u2192        # this does not work for more than 2 coordinates\\n   204\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   205\u2192        if pos == 'left':\\n   206\u2192            mat[:model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = 1\\n   207\u2192        else:\\n   208\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = 1\\n   209\u2192    else:\\n   210\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   211\u2192\\n   212\u2192        for i in range(model.n_inputs):\\n   213\u2192            mat[i, i] = 1\\n   214\u2192        if pos == 'right':\\n   215\u2192            mat = np.roll(mat, (noutp - model.n_outputs))\\n   216\u2192    return mat\\n   217\u2192\\n   218\u2192\\n   219\u2192def _cstack(left, right):\\n   220\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   221\u2192    Function corresponding to '&' operation.\\n   222\u2192\\n   223\u2192    Parameters\\n   224\u2192    ----------\\n   225\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   226\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   227\u2192\\n   228\u2192    Returns\\n   229\u2192    -------\\n   230\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   231\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   232\u2192\\n   233\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   234\u2192    noutp = _compute_n_outputs(left, right)\\n   235\u2192\\n   236\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   237\u2192        cleft = _coord_matrix(left, 'left', noutp)\\n   238\u2192    else:\\n   239\u2192        cleft = np.zeros((noutp, left.shape[1]))\\n   240\u2192        cleft[: left.shape[0], : left.shape[1]] = left\\n   241\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   242\u2192        cright = _coord_matrix(right, 'right', noutp)\\n   243\u2192    else:\\n   244\u2192        cright = np.zeros((noutp, right.shape[1]))\\n   245\u2192        cright[-right.shape[0]:, -right.shape[1]:] = 1\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192    return np.hstack([cleft, cright])\\n   248\u2192\\n   249\u2192\\n   250\u2192def _cdot(left, right):\\n   251\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   252\u2192    Function corresponding to \\\"|\\\" operation.\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192    Parameters\\n   255\u2192    ----------\\n   256\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   257\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192    Returns\\n   260\u2192    -------\\n   261\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   262\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   263\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192    left, right = right, left\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input, position):\\n   268\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   269\u2192        Return ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` for a model or coord_matrix.\\n   270\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   271\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   272\u2192            coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n   273\u2192        else:\\n   274\u2192            coords = input\\n   275\u2192        return coords\\n   276\u2192\\n   277\u2192    cleft = _n_inputs_outputs(left, 'left')\\n   278\u2192    cright = _n_inputs_outputs(right, 'right')\\n   279\u2192\\n   280\u2192    try:\\n   281\u2192        result = np.dot(cleft, cright)\\n   282\u2192    except ValueError:\\n   283\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   284\u2192            'Models cannot be combined with the \\\"|\\\" operator; '\\n   285\u2192            'left coord_matrix is {}, right coord_matrix is {}'.format(\\n   286\u2192                cright, cleft))\\n   287\u2192    return result\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192\\n   290\u2192def _separable(transform):\\n   291\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   292\u2192    Calculate the separability of outputs.\\n   293\u2192\\n   294\u2192    Parameters\\n   295\u2192    ----------\\n   296\u2192    transform : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   297\u2192        A transform (usually a compound model).\\n   298\u2192\\n   299\u2192    Returns :\\n   300\u2192    is_separable : ndarray of dtype np.bool\\n   301\u2192        An array of shape (transform.n_outputs,) of boolean type\\n   302\u2192        Each element represents the separablity of the corresponding output.\\n   303\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   304\u2192    if (transform_matrix := transform._calculate_separability_matrix()) is not NotImplemented:\\n   305\u2192        return transform_matrix\\n   306\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n   307\u2192        sepleft = _separable(transform.left)\\n   308\u2192        sepright = _separable(transform.right)\\n   309\u2192        return _operators[transform.op](sepleft, sepright)\\n   310\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, Model):\\n   311\u2192        return _coord_matrix(transform, 'left', transform.n_outputs)\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192\\n   314\u2192# Maps modeling operators to a function computing and represents the\\n   315\u2192# relationship of axes as an array of 0-es and 1-s\\n   316\u2192_operators = {'&': _cstack, '|': _cdot, '+': _arith_oper, '-': _arith_oper,\\n   317\u2192              '*': _arith_oper, '/': _arith_oper, '**': _arith_oper}\\n models.Shift(2, name=\\\"shift2\\\")\\n    rotation = models.Rotation2D(2, name=\\\"rotation\\\")\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Simple models:\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift1: {shift1.n_inputs} inputs, {shift1.n_outputs} outputs\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift2: {shift2.n_inputs} inputs, {shift2.n_outputs} outputs\\\") \\n    print(f\\\"  rotation: {rotation.n_inputs} inputs, {rotation.n_outputs} outputs\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Create a simple CompoundModel (this should work)\\n    simple_compound = shift1 & shift2\\n    print(f\\\"Simple compound (shift1 & shift2):\\\")\\n {simple_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {simple_compound.left} (type: {type(simple_compound.left)})\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {simple_compound.right} (type: {type(simple_compound.right)})\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test separability matrix for simple compound\\n    try:\\n        simple_sep_matrix = separability_matrix(simple_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"  Separability matrix for simple compound:\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"    {simple_sep_matrix}\\\")\\n        print(f\\\"    Shape: {simple_sep_matrix.shape}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"  ERROR with simple compound: {e}\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Create a nested CompoundModel (this might fail)\\n    nested_compound = simple_compound | rotation\\n    print(f\\\"Nested compound (simple_compound | rotation):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Type: {type(nested_compound)}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Is CompoundModel: {isinstance(nested_compound, CompoundModel)}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {nested_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {nested_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {nested_compound.left} (type: compound\\n    try:\\n        nested_sep_matrix = separability_matrix(nested_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"  Separability matrix for nested compound:\\\")\\n        print(f\\\" traceback\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Let's trace through the _separable function for the nested case\\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*60)\\n    print(\\\"TRACING _separable FUNCTION FOR NESTED COMPOUND\\\")\\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*60)\\n    \\n    def trace_separable(transform, depth=0):\\n        indent = \\\"  \\\" * depth\\n        print(f\\\"{indent}trace_separable({transform}, type={type(transform)})\\\")\\n        \\n        if hasattr(transform, '_calculate_separability_matrix'):\\n            matrix = transform._calculate_separability_matrix()\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  _calculate_separability_matrix() -> {matrix}\\\")\\n            if matrix is not NotImplemented:\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  returning custom matrix: {matrix}\\\")\\n                return matrix\\n        \\n        if isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  CompoundModel: op='{transform.op}'\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Left: {transform.left} (type: {type(transform.left)})\\\")\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Right: {transform.right} (type: {type(transform.right)})\\\")\\n            \\n            sepleft = trace_separable(transform.left, depth + 1)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  sepleft result: {sepleft}\\\")\\n            \\n            sepright = trace_separable(transform.right, depth + 1)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  sepright result: {sepright}\\\")\\n            \\n            from astropy.modeling.separable import _operators\\n            operator_func = _operators[transform.op]\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Using operator function: {operator_func}\\\")\\n            \\n            try:\\n                result = operator_func(sepleft, sepright)\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  operator result: {result}\\\")\\n                return result\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"{indent}  ERROR in operator: {e}\\\")\\n                raise\\n        else:\\n            from astropy.modeling.separable import _coord_matrix\\n            result = _coord_matrix(transform, 'left', transform.n_outputs)\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  _coord_matrix result: {result}\\\")\\n            return result\\n    \\n    try:\\n        result = trace_separable(nested_compound)\\n        print(f\\\"Final result: {result}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"Final error: {e}\\\")\\n        import traceback\\n model structure to test separability\\nclass Model:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimal Model class for testing\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, n_inputs, n_outputs, name=None, separable=True):\\n        self.n_inputs = n_inputs\\n        self.n_outputs = n_outputs\\n        self.name = name\\n        self.separable = separable\\n        \\n    def _calculate_separability_matrix(self):\\n        return NotImplemented\\n\\nclass CompoundModel(Model):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimal CompoundModel class for testing\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, op, left, right, name=None):\\n        self.op = op\\n        self.left = left\\n        self.right = right\\n        self.name = name\\n        \\n        # Calculate combined inputs/outputs\\n        if op == '&':  # concatenation\\n            self.n_inputs = left.n_inputs + right.n_inputs\\n            self.n_outputs = left.n_outputs + right.n_outputs\\n        elif op == '|':  # composition\\n            self.n_inputs = right.n_inputs  # input of rightmost (first applied)\\n            self.n_outputs = left.n_outputs  # output of leftmost (last applied)\\n        \\n    def _calculate_separability_matrix(self):\\n        return NotImplemented\\n\\nclass Mapping(Model):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimal Mapping class for testing\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, mapping, name=None):\\n        self.mapping = mapping\\n        self.n_inputs = max(mapping) + 1\\n        self.n_outputs = len(mapping)\\n        self.name = name\\n        self.separable = True\\n        \\n    def _calculate_separability_matrix(self):\\n separability_matrix\\n    \\n    # Create test models\\n    shift1 = Model(1, 1, \\\"shift1\\\", separable=True)\\n    shift2 = Model(1, 1, \\\"shift2\\\", separable=True)\\n    rotation = Model(2, 2, \\\"rotation\\\", separable=False)\\n    \\n    print(\\\"Test models:\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift1: {shift1.n_inputs}\u2192{shift1.n_outputs}, separable={shift1.separable}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  shift2: {shift2.n_inputs}\u2192{shift2.n_outputs}, separable={shift2.separable}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  rotation: {rotation.n_inputs}\u2192{rotation.n_outputs}, separable={rotation.separable}\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test 1: Simple compound model (shift1 & shift2)\\n    simple_compound = CompoundModel('&', shift1, shift2, \\\"simple\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Simple compound (shift1 & shift2):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {simple_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {simple_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {simple_compound.left.name}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {simple_compound.right.name}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Test separability\\n    simple_matrix = _separable(simple_compound)\\n    print(f\\\"  _separable result: {simple_matrix}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Shape: {simple_matrix.shape}\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    # Test 2: Nested compound model (simple_compound | rotation)\\n    nested_compound = CompoundModel('|', simple_compound, rotation, \\\"nested\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"Nested compound (simple | rotation):\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Inputs: {nested_compound.n_inputs}, Outputs: {nested_compound.n_outputs}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Left: {nested_compound.left.name} (type: {type(nested_compound.left)})\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"  Right: {nested_compound.right.name}\\\")\\n    \\n    # Trace the issue step by step\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nTracing _separable for nested compound:\\\")\\n    \\n    def trace_separable(transform, depth=0):\\n        indent = \\\"  \\\" * depth\\n        print(f\\\"{indent}Processing: {getattr(transform, 'name', 'unnamed')} (type: {type(transform).__name__})\\\")\\n        \\n        if hasattr(transform, '_calculate_separability_matrix'):\\n            matrix = matrix\\n        \\n        if isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  CompoundModel with op: '{transform.op}'\\\")\\n            \\n            # Get left and right separability matrices\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Processing left: {getattr(transform.left, 'name', 'unnamed')}\\\")\\n            sepleft = trace_separable(transform.left, depth + 1)\\n operator\\n            operator_func = _operators[transform.op]\\n            print(f\\\"{indent}  Applying operator '{transform.op}' using {operator_func.__name__}\\\")\\n            \\n     1\u2192# Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst\\n     2\u2192\\n     3\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     4\u2192Functions to determine if a model is separable, i.e.\\n     5\u2192if the model outputs are independent.\\n     6\u2192\\n     7\u2192It analyzes ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` and the operators\\n     8\u2192in a compound model by stepping through the transforms\\n     9\u2192and creating a ``coord_matrix`` of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192Each modeling operator is represented by a function which\\n    13\u2192takes two simple models (or two ``coord_matrix`` arrays) and\\n    14\u2192returns an array of shape (``n_outputs``, ``n_inputs``).\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192import numpy as np\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192from .core import Model, ModelDefinitionError, CompoundModel\\n    21\u2192from .mappings import Mapping\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192__all__ = [\\\"is_separable\\\", \\\"separability_matrix\\\"]\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192def is_separable(transform):\\n    28\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    29\u2192    A separability test for the outputs of a transform.\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192    Parameters\\n    32\u2192    ----------\\n    33\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    34\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192    Returns\\n    37\u2192    -------\\n    38\u2192    is_separable : ndarray\\n    39\u2192        A boolean array with size ``transform.n_outputs`` where\\n    40\u2192        each element indicates whether the output is independent\\n    41\u2192        and the result of a separable transform.\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192    Examples\\n    44\u2192    --------\\n    45\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    46\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    47\u2192        array([ True,  True]...)\\n    48\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    49\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    50\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    51\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    52\u2192        array([False, False]...)\\n    53\u2192    >>> is_separable(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    54\u2192        array([ True,  True,  True,  True]...)\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    57\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    58\u2192        is_separable = np.array([False] * transform.n_outputs).T\\n    59\u2192        return is_separable\\n    60\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n    61\u2192    is_separable = separable_matrix.sum(1)\\n    62\u2192    is_separable = np.where(is_separable != 1, False, True)\\n    63\u2192    return is_separable\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192def separability_matrix(transform):\\n    67\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    68\u2192    Compute the correlation between outputs and inputs.\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192    Parameters\\n    71\u2192    ----------\\n    72\u2192    transform : `~astropy.modeling.core.Model`\\n    73\u2192        A (compound) model.\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192    Returns\\n    76\u2192    -------\\n    77\u2192    separable_matrix : ndarray\\n    78\u2192        A boolean correlation matrix of shape (n_outputs, n_inputs).\\n    79\u2192        Indicates the dependence of outputs on inputs. For completely\\n    80\u2192        independent outputs, the diagonal elements are True and\\n    81\u2192        off-diagonal elements are False.\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192    Examples\\n    84\u2192    --------\\n    85\u2192    >>> from astropy.modeling.models import Shift, Scale, Rotation2D, Polynomial2D\\n    86\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Scale(1) & Scale(2))\\n    87\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    88\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Rotation2D(2))\\n    89\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    90\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]) | \\\\\\n    91\u2192        Polynomial2D(1) & Polynomial2D(2))\\n    92\u2192        array([[ True,  True], [ True,  True]]...)\\n    93\u2192    >>> separability_matrix(Shift(1) & Shift(2) | Mapping([0, 1, 0, 1]))\\n    94\u2192        array([[ True, False], [False,  True], [ True, False], [False,  True]]...)\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    97\u2192    if transform.n_inputs == 1 and transform.n_outputs > 1:\\n    98\u2192        return np.ones((transform.n_outputs, transform.n_inputs),\\n    99\u2192                       dtype=np.bool_)\\n   100\u2192    separable_matrix = _separable(transform)\\n   101\u2192    separable_matrix = np.where(separable_matrix != 0, True, False)\\n   102\u2192    return separable_matrix\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192def _compute_n_outputs(left, right):\\n   106\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   107\u2192    Compute the number of outputs of two models.\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192    The two models are the left and right model to an operation in\\n   110\u2192    the expression tree of a compound model.\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192    Parameters\\n   113\u2192    ----------\\n   114\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   115\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   118\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   119\u2192        lnout = left.n_outputs\\n   120\u2192    else:\\n   121\u2192        lnout = left.shape[0]\\n   122\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   123\u2192        rnout = right.n_outputs\\n   124\u2192    else:\\n   125\u2192        rnout = right.shape[0]\\n   126\u2192    noutp = lnout + rnout\\n   127\u2192    return noutp\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192def _arith_oper(left, right):\\n   131\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   132\u2192    Function corresponding to one of the arithmetic operators\\n   133\u2192    ['+', '-'. '*', '/', '**'].\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192    This always returns a nonseparable output.\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192    Parameters\\n   139\u2192    ----------\\n   140\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   141\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192    Returns\\n   144\u2192    -------\\n   145\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   146\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   147\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   148\u2192    # models have the same number of inputs and outputs\\n   149\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input):\\n   150\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   151\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.n_outputs, input.n_inputs\\n   152\u2192        else:\\n   153\u2192            n_outputs, n_inputs = input.shape\\n   154\u2192        return n_inputs, n_outputs\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192    left_inputs, left_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(left)\\n   157\u2192    right_inputs, right_outputs = _n_inputs_outputs(right)\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192    if left_inputs != right_inputs or left_outputs != right_outputs:\\n   160\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   161\u2192            \\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: left (n_inputs={}, \\\"\\n   162\u2192            \\\"n_outputs={}) and right (n_inputs={}, n_outputs={}); \\\"\\n   163\u2192            \\\"models must have the same n_inputs and the same \\\"\\n   164\u2192            \\\"n_outputs for this operator.\\\".format(\\n   165\u2192                left_inputs, left_outputs, right_inputs, right_outputs))\\n   166\u2192\\n   167\u2192    result = np.ones((left_outputs, left_inputs))\\n   168\u2192    return result\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\n   171\u2192def _coord_matrix(model, pos, noutp):\\n   172\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   173\u2192    Create an array representing inputs and outputs of a simple model.\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192    The array has a shape (noutp, model.n_inputs).\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192    Parameters\\n   178\u2192    ----------\\n   179\u2192    model : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   180\u2192        model\\n   181\u2192    pos : str\\n   182\u2192        Position of this model in the expression tree.\\n   183\u2192        One of ['left', 'right'].\\n   184\u2192    noutp : int\\n   185\u2192        Number of outputs of the compound model of which the input model\\n   186\u2192        is a left or right child.\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   189\u2192    if isinstance(model, Mapping):\\n   190\u2192        axes = []\\n   191\u2192        for i in model.mapping:\\n   192\u2192            axis = np.zeros((model.n_inputs,))\\n   193\u2192            axis[i] = 1\\n   194\u2192            axes.append(axis)\\n   195\u2192        m = np.vstack(axes)\\n   196\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   197\u2192        if pos == 'left':\\n   198\u2192            mat[: model.n_outputs, :model.n_inputs] = m\\n   199\u2192        else:\\n   200\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = m\\n   201\u2192        return mat\\n   202\u2192    if not model.separable:\\n   203\u2192        # this does not work for more than 2 coordinates\\n   204\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   205\u2192        if pos == 'left':\\n   206\u2192            mat[:model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = 1\\n   207\u2192        else:\\n   208\u2192            mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = 1\\n   209\u2192    else:\\n   210\u2192        mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))\\n   211\u2192\\n   212\u2192        for i in range(model.n_inputs):\\n   213\u2192            mat[i, i] = 1\\n   214\u2192        if pos == 'right':\\n   215\u2192            mat = np.roll(mat, (noutp - model.n_outputs))\\n   216\u2192    return mat\\n   217\u2192\\n   218\u2192\\n   219\u2192def _cstack(left, right):\\n   220\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   221\u2192    Function corresponding to '&' operation.\\n   222\u2192\\n   223\u2192    Parameters\\n   224\u2192    ----------\\n   225\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   226\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   227\u2192\\n   228\u2192    Returns\\n   229\u2192    -------\\n   230\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   231\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   232\u2192\\n   233\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   234\u2192    noutp = _compute_n_outputs(left, right)\\n   235\u2192\\n   236\u2192    if isinstance(left, Model):\\n   237\u2192        cleft = _coord_matrix(left, 'left', noutp)\\n   238\u2192    else:\\n   239\u2192        cleft = np.zeros((noutp, left.shape[1]))\\n   240\u2192        cleft[: left.shape[0], : left.shape[1]] = left\\n   241\u2192    if isinstance(right, Model):\\n   242\u2192        cright = _coord_matrix(right, 'right', noutp)\\n   243\u2192    else:\\n   244\u2192        cright = np.zeros((noutp, right.shape[1]))\\n   245\u2192        cright[-right.shape[0]:, -right.shape[1]:] = 1\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192    return np.hstack([cleft, cright])\\n   248\u2192\\n   249\u2192\\n   250\u2192def _cdot(left, right):\\n   251\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   252\u2192    Function corresponding to \\\"|\\\" operation.\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192    Parameters\\n   255\u2192    ----------\\n   256\u2192    left, right : `astropy.modeling.Model` or ndarray\\n   257\u2192        If input is of an array, it is the output of `coord_matrix`.\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192    Returns\\n   260\u2192    -------\\n   261\u2192    result : ndarray\\n   262\u2192        Result from this operation.\\n   263\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192    left, right = right, left\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192    def _n_inputs_outputs(input, position):\\n   268\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   269\u2192        Return ``n_inputs``, ``n_outputs`` for a model or coord_matrix.\\n   270\u2192        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   271\u2192        if isinstance(input, Model):\\n   272\u2192            coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n   273\u2192        else:\\n   274\u2192            coords = input\\n   275\u2192        return coords\\n   276\u2192\\n   277\u2192    cleft = _n_inputs_outputs(left, 'left')\\n   278\u2192    cright = _n_inputs_outputs(right, 'right')\\n   279\u2192\\n   280\u2192    try:\\n   281\u2192        result = np.dot(cleft, cright)\\n   282\u2192    except ValueError:\\n   283\u2192        raise ModelDefinitionError(\\n   284\u2192            'Models cannot be combined with the \\\"|\\\" operator; '\\n   285\u2192            'left coord_matrix is {}, right coord_matrix is {}'.format(\\n   286\u2192                cright, cleft))\\n   287\u2192    return result\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192\\n   290\u2192def _separable(transform):\\n   291\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   292\u2192    Calculate the separability of outputs.\\n   293\u2192\\n   294\u2192    Parameters\\n   295\u2192    ----------\\n   296\u2192    transform : `astropy.modeling.Model`\\n   297\u2192        A transform (usually a compound model).\\n   298\u2192\\n   299\u2192    Returns :\\n   300\u2192    is_separable : ndarray of dtype np.bool\\n   301\u2192        An array of shape (transform.n_outputs,) of boolean type\\n   302\u2192        Each element represents the separablity of the corresponding output.\\n   303\u2192    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   304\u2192    if (transform_matrix := transform._calculate_separability_matrix()) is not NotImplemented:\\n   305\u2192        return transform_matrix\\n   306\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, CompoundModel):\\n   307\u2192        sepleft = _separable(transform.left)\\n   308\u2192        sepright = _separable(transform.right)\\n   309\u2192        return _operators[transform.op](sepleft, sepright)\\n   310\u2192    elif isinstance(transform, Model):\\n   311\u2192        return _coord_matrix(transform, 'left', transform.n_outputs)\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192\\n   314\u2192# Maps modeling operators to a function computing and represents the\\n   315\u2192# relationship of axes as an array of 0-es and 1-s\\n   316\u2192_operators = {'&': _cstack, '|': _cdot, '+': _arith_oper, '-': _arith_oper,\\n   317\u2192              '*': _arith_oper, '/': _arith_oper, '**': _arith_oper}\\n   318\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. The _separable function (lines 290-317):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - Checks for custom _calculate_separability_matrix() first\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - For CompoundModel: recursively calls _separable on left and right\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - For simple Model: calls _coord_matrix\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"2. The _cdot function (lines 250-287):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - Handles '|' (composition) operator\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - Swaps left and right (line 265)\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - Uses _n_inputs_outputs helper function\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"3. The _n_inputs_outputs function (lines 267-275):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - For Model: calls _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\\")\\n    print(\\\"   - For array: returns input as-is\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"POTENTIAL BUG IDENTIFICATION:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"-\\\" * 30)\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"The issue is in the _n_inputs_outputs function within _cdot!\\\")\\n    print()\\n    print(\\\"Current code (line 272):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"    coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\\")\\n    print()\\n    print(\\\"PROBLEM:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- When input is a CompoundModel, it's treated as a simple Model\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- _coord_matrix is called directly instead of using _separable\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- This bypasses the recursive separability calculation for nested CompoundModels\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"DETAILED ISSUE EXPLANATION:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- For nested CompoundModels like (A & B) | C:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  1. _separable is called on the outer CompoundModel\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  2. It calls _cdot with sepleft=separability_matrix(A&B) and sepright=separability_matrix(C)\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  3. _cdot swaps them and calls _n_inputs_outputs\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  4. _n_inputs_outputs calls _coord_matrix on (A&B) directly\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  5. _coord_matrix treats (A&B) as a simple model, not as a CompoundModel\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  6. This loses the separability information of the nested structure\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- When _n_inputs_outputs receives a CompoundModel, it should use _separable\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- This would properly handle the nested structure recursively\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"THE FIX:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"-\\\" * 10)\\n    print(\\\"In _cdot function, the _n_inputs_outputs helper should check if input\\\")\\n    print(\\\"is a CompoundModel and call _separable instead of _coord_matrix:\\\")\\n    print()\\n    print(\\\"BEFORE (current buggy code):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"    if isinstance(input, Model):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"        coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\\")\\n    print()\\n    print(\\\"AFTER (fixed code):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"    if isinstance(input, Model):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"        if isinstance(input, CompoundModel):\\\")\\n    print(\\\"            coords = _separable(input)\\\")\\n    print(\\\"        else:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"            coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"VERIFICATION:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- This matches the pattern used in the main _separable function\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- It ensures CompoundModels are handled recursively\\\")\\n    print(\\\"- It preserves existing behavior for simple Models\\\")\\n    print()\\n    \\n    print(\\\"ROOT CAUSE:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"The bug occurs because _cdot doesn't distinguish between simple Models\\\")\\n    print(\\\"and CompoundModels when calling _coord_matrix. CompoundModels need\\\")\\n    print(\\\"recursive processing via _separable, not direct matrix generation.\\\")\\n    \\n    print(\\\"=\\\"*70)\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    analyze_separable_code()\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":4,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":322,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":68304,\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\"}},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Yc7gvAEjmQJNQfYYGby7xk\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"id\":\"msg_01NB4A16sSdFS1DbkFHSVSqD\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_012F4CkxCwtDeXXbAEZji1XZ\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspaces/claude-code-flow/astropy_fix/astropy/modeling/tests/test_separable.py\"}}],\"stop_reason\":\"tool_use\",\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":8,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4845,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":53874,\"output_tokens\":107,\"service_tier\":\"standard\"}},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01PBxhv96nFxSavtms7yjM7b\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_012F4CkxCwtDeXXbAEZji1XZ\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192# Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst\\n     2\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     3\u2192Test separability of models.\\n     4\u2192\\n     5\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     6\u2192# pylint: disable=invalid-name\\n     7\u2192import pytest\\n     8\u2192import numpy as np\\n     9\u2192from numpy.testing import assert_allclose\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192from astropy.modeling import custom_model, models\\n    12\u2192from astropy.modeling.models import Mapping\\n    13\u2192from astropy.modeling.separable import (_coord_matrix, is_separable, _cdot,\\n    14\u2192                                        _cstack, _arith_oper, separability_matrix)\\n    15\u2192from astropy.modeling.core import ModelDefinitionError\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192sh1 = models.Shift(1, name='shift1')\\n    19\u2192sh2 = models.Shift(2, name='sh2')\\n    20\u2192scl1 = models.Scale(1, name='scl1')\\n    21\u2192scl2 = models.Scale(2, name='scl2')\\n    22\u2192map1 = Mapping((0, 1, 0, 1), name='map1')\\n    23\u2192map2 = Mapping((0, 0, 1), name='map2')\\n    24\u2192map3 = Mapping((0, 0), name='map3')\\n    25\u2192rot = models.Rotation2D(2, name='rotation')\\n    26\u2192p2 = models.Polynomial2D(1, name='p2')\\n    27\u2192p22 = models.Polynomial2D(2, name='p22')\\n    28\u2192p1 = models.Polynomial1D(1, name='p1')\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192compound_models = {\\n    32\u2192    'cm1': (map3 & sh1 | rot & sh1 | sh1 & sh2 & sh1,\\n    33\u2192            (np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    34\u2192             np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]))\\n    35\u2192            ),\\n    36\u2192    'cm2': (sh1 & sh2 | rot | map1 | p2 & p22,\\n    37\u2192            (np.array([False, False]),\\n    38\u2192             np.array([[True, True], [True, True]]))\\n    39\u2192            ),\\n    40\u2192    'cm3': (map2 | rot & scl1,\\n    41\u2192            (np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    42\u2192             np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]))\\n    43\u2192            ),\\n    44\u2192    'cm4': (sh1 & sh2 | map2 | rot & scl1,\\n    45\u2192            (np.array([False, False, True]),\\n    46\u2192             np.array([[True, False], [True, False], [False, True]]))\\n    47\u2192            ),\\n    48\u2192    'cm5': (map3 | sh1 & sh2 | scl1 & scl2,\\n    49\u2192            (np.array([False, False]),\\n    50\u2192             np.array([[True], [True]]))\\n    51\u2192            ),\\n    52\u2192    'cm7': (map2 | p2 & sh1,\\n    53\u2192            (np.array([False, True]),\\n    54\u2192             np.array([[True, False], [False, True]]))\\n    55\u2192            )\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192\\n    59\u2192def test_coord_matrix():\\n    60\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p2, 'left', 2)\\n    61\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1, 1], [0, 0]]), c)\\n    62\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p2, 'right', 2)\\n    63\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0, 0], [1, 1]]), c)\\n    64\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p1, 'left', 2)\\n    65\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [0]]), c)\\n    66\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(p1, 'left', 1)\\n    67\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1]]), c)\\n    68\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, 'left', 2)\\n    69\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [0]]), c)\\n    70\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, 'right', 2)\\n    71\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0], [1]]), c)\\n    72\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(sh1, 'right', 3)\\n    73\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[0], [0], [1]]), c)\\n    74\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(map3, 'left', 2)\\n    75\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [1]]), c)\\n    76\u2192    c = _coord_matrix(map3, 'left', 3)\\n    77\u2192    assert_allclose(np.array([[1], [1], [0]]), c)\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192def test_cdot():\\n    81\u2192    result = _cdot(sh1, scl1)\\n    82\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1]]))\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192    result = _cdot(rot, p2)\\n    85\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2, 2]]))\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192    result = _cdot(rot, rot)\\n    88\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2, 2], [2, 2]]))\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192    result = _cdot(Mapping((0, 0)), rot)\\n    91\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[2], [2]]))\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192    with pytest.raises(ModelDefinitionError,\\n    94\u2192                       match=r\\\"Models cannot be combined with the \\\\\\\"|\\\\\\\" operator; .*\\\"):\\n    95\u2192        _cdot(sh1, map1)\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192def test_cstack():\\n    99\u2192    result = _cstack(sh1, scl1)\\n   100\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 0], [0, 1]]))\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192    result = _cstack(sh1, rot)\\n   103\u2192    assert_allclose(result,\\n   104\u2192                    np.array([[1, 0, 0],\\n   105\u2192                              [0, 1, 1],\\n   106\u2192                              [0, 1, 1]])\\n   107\u2192                    )\\n   108\u2192    result = _cstack(rot, sh1)\\n   109\u2192    assert_allclose(result,\\n   110\u2192                    np.array([[1, 1, 0],\\n   111\u2192                              [1, 1, 0],\\n   112\u2192                              [0, 0, 1]])\\n   113\u2192                    )\\n   114\u2192\\n   115\u2192\\n   116\u2192def test_arith_oper():\\n   117\u2192    # Models as inputs\\n   118\u2192    result = _arith_oper(sh1, scl1)\\n   119\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1]]))\\n   120\u2192    result = _arith_oper(rot, rot)\\n   121\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1], [1, 1]]))\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192    # ndarray\\n   124\u2192    result = _arith_oper(np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]), np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]))\\n   125\u2192    assert_allclose(result, np.array([[1, 1], [1, 1]]))\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192    # Error\\n   128\u2192    with pytest.raises(ModelDefinitionError, match=r\\\"Unsupported operands for arithmetic operator: .*\\\"):\\n   129\u2192        _arith_oper(sh1, map1)\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\n   132\u2192@pytest.mark.parametrize(('compound_model', 'result'), compound_models.values())\\n   133\u2192def test_separable(compound_model, result):\\n   134\u2192    assert_allclose(is_separable(compound_model), result[0])\\n   135\u2192    assert_allclose(separability_matrix(compound_model), result[1])\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192def test_custom_model_separable():\\n   139\u2192    @custom_model\\n   140\u2192    def model_a(x):\\n   141\u2192        return x\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192    assert model_a().separable\\n   144\u2192\\n   145\u2192    @custom_model\\n   146\u2192    def model_c(x, y):\\n   147\u2192        return x + y\\n   148\u2192\\n   149\u2192    assert not model_c().separable\\n   150\u2192    assert np.all(separability_matrix(model_c()) == [True, True])\\n   151\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01PBxhv96nFxSavtms7yjM7b\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_014kDozhYAaKmyndAf7cnqGj\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"File created successfully at: /workspaces/claude-code-flow/analyze_separable.py\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Yc7gvAEjmQJNQfYYGby7xk\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"id\":\"msg_01SDqhPdKdYmh2MHGPjF5iKr\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01W6y4sgDH2FsrUsVrAcUMFh\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"python /workspaces/claude-code-flow/analyze_separable.py\",\"description\":\"Run the code analysis to understand the bug\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":3,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1251,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":68626,\"output_tokens\":38,\"service_tier\":\"standard\"}},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Yc7gvAEjmQJNQfYYGby7xk\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01W6y4sgDH2FsrUsVrAcUMFh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"======================================================================\\nANALYSIS OF ASTROPY SEPARABILITY MATRIX BUG #12907\\n======================================================================\\n\\nCURRENT IMPLEMENTATION ANALYSIS:\\n----------------------------------------\\n\\n1. The _separable function (lines 290-317):\\n   - Checks for custom _calculate_separability_matrix() first\\n   - For CompoundModel: recursively calls _separable on left and right\\n   - For simple Model: calls _coord_matrix\\n\\n2. The _cdot function (lines 250-287):\\n   - Handles '|' (composition) operator\\n   - Swaps left and right (line 265)\\n   - Uses _n_inputs_outputs helper function\\n\\n3. The _n_inputs_outputs function (lines 267-275):\\n   - For Model: calls _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n   - For array: returns input as-is\\n\\nPOTENTIAL BUG IDENTIFICATION:\\n------------------------------\\n\\nThe issue is in the _n_inputs_outputs function within _cdot!\\n\\nCurrent code (line 272):\\n    coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n\\nPROBLEM:\\n- When input is a CompoundModel, it's treated as a simple Model\\n- _coord_matrix is called directly instead of using _separable\\n- This bypasses the recursive separability calculation for nested CompoundModels\\n\\nDETAILED ISSUE EXPLANATION:\\n- For nested CompoundModels like (A & B) | C:\\n  1. _separable is called on the outer CompoundModel\\n  2. It calls _cdot with sepleft=separability_matrix(A&B) and sepright=separability_matrix(C)\\n  3. _cdot swaps them and calls _n_inputs_outputs\\n  4. _n_inputs_outputs calls _coord_matrix on (A&B) directly\\n  5. _coord_matrix treats (A&B) as a simple model, not as a CompoundModel\\n  6. This loses the separability information of the nested structure\\n\\nEXPECTED BEHAVIOR:\\n- When _n_inputs_outputs receives a CompoundModel, it should use _separable\\n- This would properly handle the nested structure recursively\\n\\nTHE FIX:\\n----------\\nIn _cdot function, the _n_inputs_outputs helper should check if input\\nis a CompoundModel and call _separable instead of _coord_matrix:\\n\\nBEFORE (current buggy code):\\n    if isinstance(input, Model):\\n        coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n\\nAFTER (fixed code):\\n    if isinstance(input, Model):\\n        if isinstance(input, CompoundModel):\\n            coords = _separable(input)\\n        else:\\n            coords = _coord_matrix(input, position, input.n_outputs)\\n\\nVERIFICATION:\\n- This matches the pattern used in the main _separable function\\n- It ensures CompoundModels are handled recursively\\n- It preserves existing behavior for simple Models\\n\\nROOT CAUSE:\\nThe bug occurs because _cdot doesn't distinguish between simple Models\\nand CompoundModels when calling _coord_matrix. CompoundModels need\\nrecursive processing via _separable, not direct matrix generation.\\n======================================================================\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Yc7gvAEjmQJNQfYYGby7xk\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"id\":\"msg_014XbFEoCHmG7PyFi7cCz4po\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01V8npFXgExBkve2LqcfdUFk\",\"name\":\"Grep\",\"input\":{\"pattern\":\"test.*compound.*separab\",\"path\":\"/workspaces/claude-code-flow/astropy_fix/astropy/modeling/tests\",\"output_mode\":\"files_with_matches\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":3,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":838,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":69877,\"output_tokens\":2,\"service_tier\":\"standard\"}},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Yc7gvAEjmQJNQfYYGby7xk\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"id\":\"msg_014XbFEoCHmG7PyFi7cCz4po\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01SXsMpPmPbtQaUhZLce9wRR\",\"name\":\"Grep\",\"input\":{\"pattern\":\"cm.*compound\",\"path\":\"/workspaces/claude-code-flow/astropy_fix/astropy/modeling/tests/test_separable.py\",\"output_mode\":\"content\",\"-B\":2,\"-A\":5}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":3,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":838,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":69877,\"output_tokens\":2,\"service_tier\":\"standard\"}},\"parent_tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Yc7gvAEjmQJNQfYYGby7xk\",\"session_id\":\"f1ef38da-70eb-4b82-a5f9-be345c216d40\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01SXsMpPmPbtQaUhZLce9wRR\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"No     1\u2192# Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst\\n     2\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     3\u2192Test separability of models.\\n     4\u2192\\n     5\u2192\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n     6\u2192# pylint: disable=invalid-name\\n     7\u2192import pytest\\n     8\u2192import numpy as np\\n     9\u2192from numpy.testing import assert_allclose\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192from models as m\\n    10\u2192from astropy.modeling.separable import separability_matrix\\n    11\u2192import numpy as np\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192def test_nested_compound_models():\\n    14\u2192 21\u2192    print(\\\"cm = m.Linear1D(10) & m.Linear1D(5)\\\")\\n    22\u2192    result1 = separability_matrix(cm)\\n    23\u2192    print(\\\"Result:\\\")\\n    24\u2192    print(result1)\\n    25\u2192    print(\\\"Expected: diagonal matrix\\\")\\n    26\u2192    print(\\\"array([[ True, False],\\\")\\n    27\u2192    print(\\\"       [False,  True]])\\\")\\n    28\u2192    print()\\n    29\u2192    \\n    30\u2192    # Test 2: Complex model with direct combination\\n    31\u2192    print(\\\"Test 2: Complex model (Pix2Sky_TAN() & Linear1D & Linear1D)\\\")\\n    32\u2192    print(\\\"m.Pix2Sky_TAN() & m.Linear1D(10) & m.Linear1D(5)\\\")\\n    33\u2192    result2 = separability_matrix(m.Pix2Sky_TAN() & m.Linear1D(10) & m.Linear1D(5))\\n    34\u2192 41\u2192    print()\\n    42\u2192    \\n    43\u2192    # Test 3: Nested compound model (THE BUG)\\n    44\u2192    print(\\\"Test 3: Nested compound model (Pix2Sky_TAN() & cm)\\\")\\n    45\u2192 48\u2192    print(result3)\\n    49\u2192    print(\\\"Expected (should be same as Test 2):\\\")\\n    50\u2192    print(\\\"array([[ True,  True, False, False],\\\")\\n    51\u2192    print(\\\"   # Expected results\\n    61\u2192    expected1 = np.array([[True, False], [False, True]])\\n    62\u2192    expected2 = np.array([[True, True, False, False],\\n    63\u2192
